Title: SAF-004 design doc â€” Stage 4 formal spec omits command chain separator handling
Description: Section 5 Stage 4 specification only says 'Split on whitespace respecting single and double quotes.' The requirement to split on ;, &&, and || and run Stage 5 on each resulting segment is only stated in Section 9.6 as a buried implementation requirement. An implementer following the normative Section 5 spec would build a tokenizer that misses chained commands like 'git status; rm -rf .'
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Read Section 5 Stage 4 spec only. 2. Implement tokenizer per that spec. 3. Run test T-043 (git status; rm -rf . â†’ deny). 4. Test fails because ; is not treated as a command separator.
Expected Behaviour: Section 5 Stage 4 spec explicitly states that ;, && and || are treated as command chain separators and each segment is analyzed independently.
Actual Behaviour: Stage 4 spec is silent on chain separators; the requirement only appears in Section 9.6 edge cases.
Comments: Discovered during Tester review on 2026-03-10. Fix: add chain-separator rule to Section 5 Stage 4 normative spec.

Title: SAF-004 design doc â€” escape hatch residual checks (Section 12.4) don't cover full pre-scan catalogue
Description: Section 12.4 lists residual safety checks for exception-listed commands as: (1) No interpreter-chaining flags P-01 to P-09, (2) No encoded commands P-10, (3) Zone check on paths. Patterns P-11 to P-28 (IEX, Invoke-Expression, Start-Process, & $var, [Convert]::FromBase64String, pipe-to-interpreter, backtick subshell, $() subshell, eval, exec, source, dot-source, execution-policy bypass, Invoke-Item, Set-Alias, New-Alias, process substitution) are not blocked by residual checks. A broad exception pattern could allow these vectors.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Create an exception entry matching 'curl https://api.example.com'. 2. Submit command: 'curl https://api.example.com; eval $payload'. 3. Command matches exception (if pattern allows semicolons), residual check passes (eval is not in P-01 to P-10), zone check passes. Command would reach 'ask' instead of 'deny'.
Expected Behaviour: All pre-scan patterns P-01 to P-28 are applied as residual checks; no exception can override any pattern in _OBFUSCATION_PATTERNS.
Actual Behaviour: Only P-01 to P-10 are in residual checks; P-11 to P-28 can be bypassed via exception entry.
Comments: Discovered during Tester review on 2026-03-10. Fix: replace enumeration in Section 12.4 with 'apply full _OBFUSCATION_PATTERNS list to exception-matched commands.'

Title: SAF-004 design doc â€” P-10 implementation reference code not updated with Section 9.4 fix

The prose provides a corrected pattern. However the implementation reference code block at the end of Section 6 still shows the original incomplete pattern. An implementer using Section 6 as the authoritative source would implement the vulnerable version.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Copy _OBFUSCATION_PATTERNS code block from Section 6. 2. Run test T-050 (powershell -e BASE64 â†’ deny). 3. Test fails because the P-10 pattern does not match -e.
Expected Behaviour: Section 6 code block contains the corrected P-10 pattern from Section 9.4.
Actual Behaviour: Section 6 code block still has the original P-10 pattern lacking -e coverage.
Comments: Discovered during Tester review on 2026-03-10. Fix: update Section 6 code block with corrected P-10 pattern.

Title: SAF-004 design doc â€” pre-scan pattern count in Section 5 is wrong

Comments: Discovered during Tester review on 2026-03-10. Fix: update Section 5 to reference 28 patterns or consolidate all patterns into Section 6 and remove split definitions.

Title: echo/write-host/write-output shell redirect to restricted zone returns ask instead of deny
Description: Commands with path_args_restricted=False (echo write-host write-output) do not zone-check any token in their argument list. A shell redirect operator > followed by a deny-zone path (e.g. 'echo evil > .github/hooks/scripts/security_gate.py') returns ask instead of deny because the .github token is never passed to zone_classifier.
Steps to Reproduce: Call sanitize_terminal_command('echo evil > .github/hooks/scripts/security_gate.py', '/workspace'). Observe result == ('ask' None). Expected: ('deny' reason). Reproduce with test_bypass_echo_redirect_to_restricted_zone in tests/SAF-005/test_saf005_edge_cases.py.
Expected Behaviour: sanitize_terminal_command returns deny for any command that redirects output to a deny-zone path regardless of primary verb.
Actual Behaviour: Returns ask â€” path_args_restricted=False skips all path validation for echo/write-host/write-output.
Comments: Fix: after tokenization scan each segment for '>' and '>>' tokens and zone-check the immediately following token regardless of path_args_restricted. Failing tests: test_bypass_echo_redirect_to_restricted_zone test_bypass_write_output_redirect_to_restricted_zone test_bypass_write_host_redirect_to_restricted_zone test_bypass_echo_redirect_to_vscode.

Title: npm --prefix path to restricted zone not zone-checked

Steps to Reproduce: Call sanitize_terminal_command('npm install --prefix .github/node_modules requests' '/workspace'). Observe result == ('ask' None). Reproduce with test_bypass_npm_prefix_to_restricted_zone in tests/SAF-005/test_saf005_edge_cases.py.
Expected Behaviour: Returns deny for npm/yarn/pnpm with destination flag pointing to deny-zone path.
Actual Behaviour: Returns ask â€” path_args_restricted=False skips zone checks for all arguments.
Comments: Fix: detect --prefix/--cwd flags and zone-check next argument. Or set path_args_restricted=True for npm/yarn/pnpm.

Title: allow_arbitrary_paths field in CommandRule is never read by _validate_args

Title: No-space and fd-prefixed shell redirect bypasses zone check in _validate_args
Description: Step 6 of _validate_args detects redirect operators by checking whether a token equals '>' or '>>' (using _REDIRECT_TOKENS = frozenset({'>>','>'})). Shell syntax allows two additional forms that produce non-matching tokens: (1) No-space redirect: 'echo evil>.github/file' â€” shlex tokenizes this as one token 'evil>.github/file'; no standalone '>' appears (2) fd-prefixed redirect: 'echo evil 1>.github/file' â€” tokenized as '1>.github/file'; '1>' does not equal '>'. In both forms the redirect destination path is never passed to zone_classifier and the command returns 'ask' instead of 'deny'.
Steps to Reproduce: Call sanitize_terminal_command('echo evil>.github/security_gate.py', '/workspace'). Observe result == ('ask', None). Repeat with '1>.github/file' variant. Reproduce with tests ET-027 to ET-032 in tests/SAF-005/test_saf005_edge_cases.py.
Expected Behaviour: sanitize_terminal_command returns deny for any command that redirects output to a deny-zone path regardless of redirect syntax variant.
Actual Behaviour: Returns ask â€” no-space and fd-prefixed redirect tokens do not match _REDIRECT_TOKENS = frozenset({'>>'
Fixed In WP: '>'}) so the destination path is never zone-checked.
Comments: SAF-005,Discovered during Tester re-review (Iteration 2) on 2026-03-11. Fix: replace exact-token matching in Step 6 with a regex that matches (a) standalone redirect tokens optionally preceded by a file descriptor digit (e.g. re.match(r'^[0-9]*>>?$', tok)) and (b) embedded redirects where '>' appears inside a token (split on '>' and zone-check the right-hand portion). Failing tests: test_redirect_no_space_basic_bypasses_gate, test_redirect_no_space_append_bypasses_gate, test_redirect_fd_prefixed_basic_bypasses_gate, test_redirect_fd_prefixed_stderr_bypasses_gate, test_redirect_fd_prefixed_append_bypasses_gate, test_redirect_cat_fd_to_restricted_zone.

Title: dir /s with no explicit directory argument not denied â€” Windows flags bypass Step 7 cwd fallback
Description: Step 7 of _validate_args() collects path arguments by skipping tokens that start with '-' (POSIX flags). It does not skip Windows-style flags starting with '/' (e.g. /s /b for dir). For 'dir /s' with no explicit directory path args = ['/s']. '/s' is added to path_args; _is_ancestor_of_deny_zone('/s' '/workspace') returns False; the cwd fallback ('if not path_args: path_args=['.']') never fires; the command passes as 'ask' instead of 'deny'. An agent could enumerate the entire workspace recursively including .github .vscode NoAgentZone with no deny decision.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Call sanitize_terminal_command('dir /s' '/workspace'). 2. Observe result == ('ask' None). 3. Reproduce with test_dir_slash_s_blocked and test_dir_slash_s_no_path_blocked.
Expected Behaviour: sanitize_terminal_command returns ('deny' reason) for dir /s with no explicit directory argument.
Actual Behaviour: Returns ('ask' None) â€” /s is treated as a path argument; _is_ancestor_of_deny_zone('/s' ...) returns False; workspace-root ancestor check skipped.
Comments: Fixed in SAF-006 Iteration 4: _WIN_FLAG_RE = re.compile(r'^/[a-zA-Z0-9]{1,2}$') added in Step 7 path-args loop to skip Windows-style flags. All 82 SAF-006 tests pass. Verified by Tester Agent 2026-03-12.

Title: FIX-008 Popen sentinel does not block 'code-insiders' VS Code Insiders binary
Description: The _subprocess_popen_sentinel fixture in tests/conftest.py only checks for 'code' (exact match) or paths ending in os.sep+'code' or containing 'visual studio code'. The VS Code Insiders binary 'code-insiders' is not matched by any of these patterns and passes straight through to the real subprocess.Popen. On a machine where VS Code Insiders is installed this would spawn a VS Code Insiders window during tests â€” exactly the scenario Layer 3 is meant to prevent.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Create a test that calls subprocess.Popen(['code-insiders', '/tmp']). 2. Expect RuntimeError('SAFETY VIOLATION'). 3. Run test â€” observe FileNotFoundError instead (sentinel did not intercept). 4. See tests/FIX-008/test_fix008_multilayer_guard.py::test_popen_sentinel_blocks_code_insiders and ::test_popen_sentinel_blocks_code_insiders_string_form.
Expected Behaviour: _subprocess_popen_sentinel raises RuntimeError('SAFETY VIOLATION') for both 'code-insiders' list form and 'code-insiders ...' string form.
Actual Behaviour: Sentinel passes 'code-insiders' through to real Popen; FileNotFoundError raised only because the binary is not installed on this machine. A machine with VS Code Insiders would silently spawn the IDE.
Comments: Discovered Tester Agent 2026-03-13. Fix: extend _guarded_popen in tests/conftest.py to also intercept 'code-insiders' in both list form (args[0] == 'code-insiders' or path ending in os.sep+'code-insiders') and string form (first word == 'code-insiders'). Also consider blocking all cmd patterns where cmd starts with 'code' to future-proof against new VS Code variants.

Title: CI Windows Build artifact path does not match Inno Setup output directory

The artifact upload will fail with No files were found with the provided path on every CI run.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Check .github/workflows/release.yml upload-artifact step path value. 2. Note it is Output/AgentEnvironmentLauncher-Setup.exe. 3. Check src/installer/windows/setup.iss â€” no OutputDir directive. 4. Inno Setup default: Output/ relative to script dir = src/installer/windows/Output/. 5. Run tester edge-case test test_artifact_path_matches_inno_setup_output_directory â€” FAIL.
Expected Behaviour: upload-artifact path is src/installer/windows/Output/AgentEnvironmentLauncher-Setup.exe matching the actual Inno Setup output location.
Actual Behaviour: Artifact path is Output/AgentEnvironmentLauncher-Setup.exe which does not exist â€” CI upload will fail.
Comments: Discovered Tester Agent 2026-03-14 INS-014 Iteration 1. Fixed in Iteration 2: path corrected to src/installer/windows/Output/AgentEnvironmentLauncher-Setup.exe; developer test strengthened to exact equality. Verified by Tester Agent 2026-03-14 â€” all 27 INS-014 tests pass; 1710 full suite pass; BUG-034 closed.

Expected Behaviour: All CI build jobs find launcher.spec and run pyinstaller successfully.
Actual Behaviour: All 4 CI build jobs fail with ERROR: Spec file "launcher.spec" not found! because launcher.spec is untracked from git.
Comments: Discovered Developer Agent 2026-03-14. Root cause: FIX-005 untracked launcher.spec to satisfy INS-012 test but launcher.spec is a manually-maintained PyInstaller spec file not an auto-generated one. Fix: add !launcher.spec negation to .gitignore and re-track with git add -f.

Title: build_dmg.sh passes --target-arch to PyInstaller with a .spec file
Description: build_dmg.sh passes --target-arch as a CLI flag but PyInstaller does not allow makespec options when a .spec file is given. The macOS ARM CI build always fails.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Push a version tag. 2. Observe macos-arm-build job failing with: ERROR: option(s) not allowed: --target-architecture/--target-arch makespec options not valid when a .spec file is given.
Expected Behaviour: PyInstaller runs without --target-arch flag when a .spec file is provided; macos-arm-build succeeds.
Actual Behaviour: PyInstaller invocation in build_dmg.sh includes --target-arch flag; this is rejected when a .spec file is given.
Comments: Discovered Developer Agent 2026-03-14 during CI run analysis. Fix: remove --target-arch line from PyInstaller invocation in build_dmg.sh.

Title: setup.iss ArchitecturesInstallMode directive not recognized by CI Inno Setup
Description: setup.iss uses ArchitecturesInstallMode=x64compatible which is not recognized by the Inno Setup version installed via chocolatey on GitHub Actions. Windows CI build fails during iscc compilation.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Push a version tag. 2. Observe windows-build job failing with: Error on line 23 in setup.iss: Unrecognized [Setup] section directive ArchitecturesInstallMode.
Expected Behaviour: iscc compiles setup.iss without errors; windows-build job succeeds.
Actual Behaviour: iscc fails with unrecognized directive error; ArchitecturesInstallMode=x64compatible and ArchitecturesAllowed=x64compatible not supported by CI Inno Setup.
Comments: Discovered Developer Agent 2026-03-14 during CI run analysis. Fix: remove ArchitecturesAllowed and ArchitecturesInstallMode lines from setup.iss.

Title: Template dropdown shows "CTkOptionMenu" instead of project types
Description: TEMPLATES_DIR in config.py uses Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent.parent / "templates" which resolves outside the PyInstaller _MEIPASS directory in bundled builds. In PyInstaller, config.py is at _MEIPASS/launcher/config.py, so .parent.parent.parent goes ABOVE _MEIPASS to the OS temp directory. The bundled templates/ directory is at _MEIPASS/templates/. Since list_templates() returns an empty list, CTkOptionMenu gets values=[] and displays its class name "CTkOptionMenu" as default text. The dropdown is non-functional — users cannot select any project type.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Install the v1.0.0 bundled app. 2. Open the launcher. 3. Observe dropdown shows "CTkOptionMenu". 4. Cannot select any project type.
Expected Behaviour: Dropdown shows available template types ("Coding", "Creative Marketing")
Actual Behaviour: Dropdown shows "CTkOptionMenu" and is non-functional
Comments: Root cause: no sys._MEIPASS handling in config.py for PyInstaller bundled paths.

Title: TS Logo stretched — hardcoded CTkImage size ignores aspect ratio
Description: CTkImage in app.py uses hardcoded size=(160, 50) which does not match the logo actual aspect ratio, causing it to appear stretched/distorted.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Open the launcher. 2. Observe the TS-Logo at the top of the window. 3. Logo appears stretched horizontally.
Expected Behaviour: Logo displayed with correct aspect ratio
Actual Behaviour: Logo appears stretched/distorted due to hardcoded dimensions
Comments: Discovered during v1.0.1 testing on Windows 11 x86.

Title: TS Logo not used as app icon — launcher.spec uses .png instead of .ico
Description: launcher.spec sets icon=TS-Logo.png but PyInstaller on Windows requires a .ico file. The built .exe uses the default Python icon instead of the TS logo.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Build the launcher with PyInstaller on Windows. 2. Check the .exe icon in file explorer or taskbar. 3. Default Python icon is shown instead of TS logo.
Expected Behaviour: .exe file shows TS logo as its icon
Actual Behaviour: .exe shows default Python/PyInstaller icon
Comments: Discovered during v1.0.1 testing on Windows 11 x86.

Title: INS-005 test_uninstall_delete_type_is_filesandirs uses wrong expected value
Description: tests/INS-005/test_ins005_edge_cases.py line 198 expects regex Type:\s*filesandirs but setup.iss correctly uses filesandordirs (valid Inno Setup syntax). The test has an incorrect expected value and fails on every full suite run.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Run .venv\Scripts\python -m pytest tests/INS-005/ -v. 2. Observe test_uninstall_delete_type_is_filesandirs fails.
Expected Behaviour: Test passes — expected regex updated to filesandordirs.
Actual Behaviour: Test expects filesandirs (invalid Inno Setup) but actual file uses filesandordirs (correct syntax). Pre-existing regression unrelated to FIX-016.
Comments: Discovered Tester Agent 2026-03-16 during FIX-016 regression run. The correct Inno Setup UninstallDelete type is filesandordirs not filesandirs.

Title: Auto-update fails — unauthenticated GitHub API returns 404 for private repo
Description: updater.py and downloader.py make unauthenticated requests to the GitHub Releases API. Since the repository is private, the API returns HTTP 404. check_for_update silently catches the exception and returns (False, current_version), so the launcher never detects available updates. The download function would also fail with a RuntimeError.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Install v1.0.1 of the launcher from the private repo. 2. Push v1.0.2 release. 3. Open the launcher. 4. No update banner appears. 5. Click "Check for Updates" — no update found.
Expected Behaviour: Launcher detects v1.0.2 is available and shows the update banner.
Actual Behaviour: Launcher reports no update available because the API call fails silently with 404.
Comments: Root cause: unauthenticated API calls to api.github.com for a private repo return 404. Fix: use gh auth token (GitHub CLI) or GITHUB_TOKEN env var for authentication.

Title: get_github_token() does not validate subprocess return code
Description: get_github_token() in github_auth.py checks result.stdout.strip() for truthiness but never checks result.returncode. If gh auth token exits with a non-zero code (e.g. not authenticated) but writes any text to stdout, that text is returned as the token value and injected into an Authorization header as-is.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Patch subprocess.run to return returncode=1 with stdout='error: not logged in...'. 2. Call get_github_token(). 3. Observe it returns the error text instead of None.
Expected Behaviour: Returns None when result.returncode != 0, regardless of stdout content.
Actual Behaviour: Returns error text as a token when returncode != 0 and stdout is non-empty. Test: test_nonzero_exit_with_error_text_on_stdout_returns_none fails.
Comments: Discovered by Tester Agent 2026-03-16 (FIX-018 Iteration 1). Fix: add if result.returncode == 0: guard before using result.stdout.

Title: Bare tilde (~) not recognised as path-like — bypasses zone check for delete commands
Description: The _is_path_like() helper returns False for tokens that have no forward slash backslash .. or leading dot. The bare tilde character (~) has none of these features so rm ~ or remove-item ~ passes _validate_args without any zone check. On Unix/macOS the shell expands ~ to $HOME before execution; on Windows PowerShell ~ also expands to HOME. An agent could therefore delete their home directory with rm ~ or remove-item ~ without triggering a deny decision.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Call sanitize_terminal_command('rm ~' 'c:/workspace'). 2. Observe result == ('allow' None). 3. On Linux/macOS: run rm ~ in a terminal — deletes users home directory symlink or files.
Expected Behaviour: sanitize_terminal_command('rm ~' 'c:/workspace') returns ('deny' '...')
Actual Behaviour: Returns ('allow' None) because ~ has no slash backslash .. or leading dot so _is_path_like returns False and no zone check is performed.
Comments: Discovered by Tester Agent during SAF-016 review 2026-03-16. Test test_rm_bare_tilde_current_behaviour in tests/SAF-016/test_saf016_tester_edge_cases.py documents the current behaviour. Recommendation: extend _is_path_like to also return True for bare ~ and tilde-prefixed paths (e.g. ~/path which already has a slash but ~ alone does not).

Title: python -m pip install bypasses VIRTUAL_ENV check
Description: In _validate_args the VIRTUAL_ENV guard is gated on verb in ("pip" "pip3"). When pip is invoked as python -m pip install the verb is "python" so the guard is never reached. pip is in _PYTHON_ALLOWED_MODULES and the subsequent args are not path-like so no deny is triggered. The command returns ("allow" None) even with no VIRTUAL_ENV set.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Remove VIRTUAL_ENV from environment. 2. Call sanitize_terminal_command("python -m pip install requests" "c:/workspace"). 3. Observe result == ("allow" None).
Expected Behaviour: sanitize_terminal_command returns ("deny" ...) for python -m pip install when no VIRTUAL_ENV is set.
Actual Behaviour: Returns ("allow" None) — venv guard never fires when verb is python. Global pip install is allowed.
Comments: Discovered by Tester Agent 2026-03-16. Fix: in _validate_args inside the -m module handler add a VIRTUAL_ENV check when module is pip or pip3 and the subcommand is install. Also fix the boundary check in BUG-050 at the same time.

Title: VIRTUAL_ENV startswith check has path-component prefix-collision
Description: The VIRTUAL_ENV check uses norm_venv.startswith(ws_root) — a raw Python string prefix test. When ws_root="c:/workspace" a VIRTUAL_ENV of "c:/workspace2/.venv" satisfies the startswith check (True) even though c:/workspace2 is a completely different directory. An attacker who controls VIRTUAL_ENV could set it to a path that shares a string prefix with the workspace root but is actually outside it.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Set ws_root="c:/workspace". 2. Set VIRTUAL_ENV="c:/workspace2/.venv". 3. Call sanitize_terminal_command("pip install requests" "c:/workspace"). 4. Observe result == ("allow" None).
Expected Behaviour: sanitize_terminal_command returns ("deny" ...) because c:/workspace2 is not inside c:/workspace.
Actual Behaviour: Returns ("allow" None) — string prefix match is not path-component-safe.
Comments: Discovered by Tester Agent 2026-03-16. Fix: norm_venv.startswith(ws_root.rstrip("/") + "/") or norm_venv == ws_root.rstrip("/") to enforce path-component boundary.

Title: SAF-020: bracket expression wildcards bypass wildcard deny check
Description: _wildcard_prefix_matches_deny_zone() only checks for * and ? wildcard characters. Unix/PowerShell bracket expressions like [.g]* [Nn]* [.v]* are valid glob patterns that expand to .github NoAgentZone and .vscode respectively. Because [ is not treated as a wildcard trigger the prefix before * is [.g] (not .g) and no deny zone starts with [ so the check returns False (allow).
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Run: sg._wildcard_prefix_matches_deny_zone("[.g]*") ? returns False. 2. Run: sanitize_terminal_command("ls [.g]*" "c:/workspace") ? returns allow. 3. In a real shell: ls [.g]* expands to .github/ contents.
Expected Behaviour: [.g]* [Nn]* [.v]* and any other bracket expression targeting deny zones must be blocked (return deny).
Actual Behaviour: All bracket-expression wildcard patterns against deny zones are allowed ? active bypass vector for all three deny zones.
Comments: Discovered by Tester Agent 2026-03-16. Fix: in _wildcard_prefix_matches_deny_zone treat [ as a wildcard character. When a bracket expression is found at root level (no safe parent entered) conservatively deny. Alternatively strip the bracket expression entirely and treat the prefix before [ as the match prefix. Combined pipe+bracket bypass also noted: ls | cat [.g]* returns allow (BUG-051 is the root cause).

Title: replace_template_placeholders raises PermissionError on read-only .md file with placeholder
Description: replace_template_placeholders() catches OSError on file read (binary-safety guard) but does NOT wrap the write_text() call. If a .md file is read-only and contains placeholder tokens, the read succeeds, replacement produces updated content, and write_text() then raises PermissionError. The function propagates the exception uncaught, causing create_project() to abort.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Create a .md file with {{PROJECT_NAME}} content. 2. Set file permissions to read-only (Windows: stat.S_IREAD). 3. Call replace_template_placeholders(). 4. PermissionError raised on write_text().
Expected Behaviour: Read-only .md files containing placeholders are silently skipped (consistent with the binary-safety guard behaviour already implemented); no exception propagates.
Actual Behaviour: PermissionError propagates from write_text(); create_project() aborts if any template .md file is read-only and contains a placeholder.
Comments: Discovered by Tester Agent during DOC-001 review 2026-03-17. Confirmed via test TST-1580 (xfail). Low real-world impact ? template files are unlikely to be read-only ? but the asymmetry between read-guard and write-guard is a latent defect. Recommend wrapping write_text() in try/except OSError to match read guard.

Title: Project README template files contain stray German session content and broken H1 heading
Description: Both Default-Project/Project/README.md and templates/coding/Project/README.md are prefixed with a German Mathematik Demo (Streamlit) README. The {{PROJECT_NAME}} placeholder begins on the same line as the last bullet point of that content (no preceding newline), so it is NOT a valid Markdown H1 heading. After placeholder replacement the project name appears mid-line rather than as a proper heading, and the German content persists in every created project.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Open Default-Project/Project/README.md. 2. Observe first line is # Mathematik Demo (Streamlit). 3. Search for # {{PROJECT_NAME}} ? found embedded at end of bullet line 27 with no leading newline.
Expected Behaviour: Both README files must begin with # {{PROJECT_NAME}} as the first standalone H1 heading. No German or session-specific content should exist in a project template file.
Actual Behaviour: First H1 is # Mathematik Demo (Streamlit). # {{PROJECT_NAME}} is embedded mid-line and is NOT a proper Markdown heading. All created projects inherit the German content unchanged.
Comments: Discovered by Tester Agent 2026-03-17 during DOC-004 review. Root cause: stray session content was written into the template without a trailing newline separator, then DOC-004 developer performed a text-only substitution without inspecting file structure. Fix: strip lines 1-27 (German section) from both README files so the file begins with # {{PROJECT_NAME}} on its own line.

Title: macOS app crash on launch — unsigned PyInstaller bundle fails Gatekeeper on macOS 14+ M3
Description: AgentEnvironmentLauncher.app v2.0.0 fails to launch on macOS 14.3 (M3 arm64). The PyInstaller bundle has no code signature. macOS 14+ Gatekeeper requires at minimum ad-hoc code signing for apps to execute. Errors: (1) 'code has no resources but signature indicates they must be present' — code signature validation failure; (2) 'bundle format unrecognized, invalid, or unsuitable — subcomponent python3.11 not properly signed'; (3) 'Launchd job spawn failed (Error 153)' via open command; (4) 'zsh: killed' on direct binary execution. build_dmg.sh has no codesign step after .app bundle assembly; launcher.spec sets codesign_identity=None.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Download AgentEnvironmentLauncher-2.0.0-arm64.dmg. 2. Mount DMG and drag .app to /Applications. 3. Double-click .app in Finder. 4. Observe: error dialog 'The application cannot be opened for an unexpected reason'. 5. Run: codesign -v AgentEnvironmentLauncher.app — observe 'code object is not signed at all'.
Expected Behaviour: App launches successfully — main GUI window appears.
Actual Behaviour: App terminates immediately. Gatekeeper kills the process with Launchd job spawn failed Error 153. codesign reports the bundle is not signed at all.
Comments: Root cause: build_dmg.sh has no codesign step after .app bundle creation. launcher.spec codesign_identity=None. All embedded binaries including python3.11 framework are unsigned. macOS 14+ Apple Silicon enforces minimum ad-hoc signing. Fixed by FIX-028 (ad-hoc codesign) and FIX-029 (CI verification).

Title: Search tools universally blocked in project folder
Description: validate_grep_search() returns deny when no filePath field is present in the payload — grep_search never provides filePath so it is always denied even when includePattern correctly scopes to the project folder. file_search goes through _EXEMPT_TOOLS but also requires a path field via extract_path() which it never provides — always returns None causing deny. validate_semantic_search() is hard-coded to always return deny with no exception for project-folder scope. Covers R1 R2 R3 from Security Verification Report.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Use grep_search with includePattern scoped to project folder. 2. Observe: denied. 3. Use file_search with any query. 4. Observe: denied. 5. Use semantic_search with any query. 6. Observe: denied.
Expected Behaviour: All three search tools work when scoped to or targeting the project folder.
Actual Behaviour: All three search tools are universally blocked regardless of scope.
Comments: Covers verification report issues R1 R2 R3. Root cause: validate_grep_search falls through to extract_path which returns None for grep_search payloads; file_search has no path field; validate_semantic_search always returns deny. Fixed in FIX-021: validate_grep_search returns allow when no path present; file_search handler added to decide(); validate_semantic_search returns allow. Tester PASS 2026-03-17.

Title: Python/pip/venv terminal commands blocked in project folder
Description: python src/app.py and python -m pytest with relative paths are denied because the security gate resolves relative paths against the workspace root making src/app.py resolve to ws_root/src/app.py which is outside the detected project folder. pip install is blocked if VIRTUAL_ENV is not set or does not point inside the project folder. Venv activation scripts (activate activate.bat Activate.ps1) are not in the command allowlist so activation is unconditionally denied. Covers R4 R7 R8 from Security Verification Report.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Run: python src/app.py from inside project folder. 2. Observe: denied. 3. Run: python -m pytest tests/. 4. Observe: denied. 5. Run: .\\venv\\Scripts\\activate. 6. Observe: denied (activate script not in allowlist).
Expected Behaviour: Python execution pip install and venv activation all work inside the project folder.
Actual Behaviour: All python/pip/venv commands are blocked due to relative path resolution against workspace root and missing allowlist entries for activation scripts.
Comments: Covers verification report issues R4 R7 R8. Root cause: relative paths resolve to ws_root/path not project_folder/path; activate scripts absent from allowlist.

Title: .venv directory creation blocked by dot-prefix rule
Description: python -m venv .venv is denied because .venv starts with a dot so _is_path_like() returns True and the resolver joins .venv with ws_root producing ws_root/.venv which is outside the project folder. The zone classifier denies it because ws_root/.venv is not inside the detected project subfolder. Covers R6 from Security Verification Report.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. cd into project folder. 2. Run: python -m venv .venv. 3. Observe: denied. Root cause traced to _check_path_arg resolving .venv to workspace root level.
Expected Behaviour: python -m venv .venv creates a virtual environment inside the project folder.
Actual Behaviour: .venv is denied because dot-prefixed relative paths are resolved against the workspace root not relative to the project folder cwd.
Comments: Covers verification report issue R6. Root cause: .venv treated as relative to workspace root not to the project folder.

Title: File tools with absolute project paths blocked inconsistently
Description: list_dir read_file create_file and replace_string_in_file with absolute paths to the project folder exhibit inconsistent behavior. First call may succeed but subsequent calls are blocked. The SAF-008 integrity check in verify_file_integrity() fails if settings.json or security_gate.py is modified after initial setup — once integrity fails all tool calls are denied for the lifetime of that hook invocation. Additionally certain absolute Windows path formats may produce inconsistent zone classification results. Covers R9 R10 R11 R12 from Security Verification Report.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Call list_dir with absolute path to project folder. 2. First call succeeds. 3. Modify any file in the project. 4. Call list_dir again with same absolute path. 5. Second call may be denied if integrity hash becomes stale.
Expected Behaviour: All file tools reliably return allow for any valid absolute path inside the project folder on every call.
Actual Behaviour: File tools return allow on first call but denied on subsequent calls after security gate files or settings are updated.
Comments: Covers verification report issues R9 R10 R11 R12. Root cause: SAF-008 integrity hash becomes stale after legitimate file modifications; absolute path normalization edge cases.

Title: cat and type terminal path resolution failures in project folder
Description: cat and type are present in the terminal command allowlist (Category G with path_args_restricted=True) but are reported as blocked in the Security Verification Report. The actual failure is that relative path arguments (e.g. cat src/app.py) are resolved against the workspace root producing ws_root/src/app.py which is outside the project folder and denied. The root cause is shared with BUG-055. Covers R5 R15 from Security Verification Report.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Run: cat src/app.py from terminal inside project folder. 2. Observe: denied. 3. Run: type file.txt. 4. Observe: denied.
Expected Behaviour: cat src/app.py and type file.txt work for files inside the project folder.
Actual Behaviour: cat and type commands targeting project folder files are denied because relative path args resolve to workspace root level.
Comments: Covers verification report issues R5 R15. Root cause: same relative path resolution issue as BUG-055 — relative paths resolve to ws_root/path rather than project_folder/path.

Title: get_errors blocked for project folder file paths
Description: validate_get_errors() zone-checks each path in the filePaths array. When the path is a relative path like src/app.py the zone classifier resolves it against ws_root producing ws_root/src/app.py which is not inside the project folder — denied. For absolute paths the classification should work in principle but path normalization issues may still produce deny. The implementation allows empty filePaths (global scope) but blocks populated arrays even when all paths are inside the project folder. Covers R13 from Security Verification Report.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Call get_errors with filePaths containing a relative project path such as .\\MyProject\\src\\app.py. 2. Observe: denied. 3. Call get_errors with no filePaths argument. 4. Observe: allowed.
Expected Behaviour: get_errors with project folder file paths returns allow.
Actual Behaviour: get_errors is denied when filePaths array contains project folder paths due to relative path resolution failure.
Comments: Covers verification report issue R13. Root cause: relative path resolution against workspace root instead of project folder context.

Title: Terminal commands with absolute project folder paths blocked
Description: When agents use absolute Windows paths in terminal commands (e.g. Get-ChildItem E:\\workspace\\TS-SAE-Project\\MyProject\\src) the zone classification may incorrectly deny. The zone_classifier Method 2 allow_pattern uses a simple regex search for the project folder name as a substring. If the workspace root directory name contains the project folder name as a component (e.g. ts-sae-myproject containing myproject) the regex may match the wrong path segment. Also absolute path tokenization through shlex may lose backslashes or produce unexpected token formats. Covers R14 from Security Verification Report.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Run: Get-ChildItem E:\\workspace\\TS-SAE-MyProject\\MyProject\\src in terminal. 2. Observe: denied (should be allowed).
Expected Behaviour: Terminal commands with absolute project folder paths return allow.
Actual Behaviour: Terminal commands using absolute paths to the project folder are blocked due to substring regex matching producing false positive or negative zone decisions.
Comments: Covers verification report issue R14. Root cause: zone_classifier Method 2 regex may match project folder name in parent directory names; absolute Windows path backslash tokenization edge cases.

Title: INS-015 test_macos_arm_has_5_steps stale after FIX-029 adds Verify Code Signing step
Description: tests/INS-015/test_ins015_macos_build_jobs.py::test_macos_arm_has_5_steps hard-codes the expected step count as 5. FIX-029 correctly added a 6th step (Verify Code Signing) to the macos-arm-build job making the test stale.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Apply FIX-029 branch. 2. Run python -m pytest tests/INS-015/test_ins015_macos_build_jobs.py::test_macos_arm_has_5_steps. 3. AssertionError: Expected 5 steps got 6.
Expected Behaviour: test_macos_arm_has_5_steps passes with assert len(arm_steps) == 6.
Actual Behaviour: AssertionError: Expected 5 steps got 6 - test hard-codes the old step count.
Comments: Discovered Tester Agent 2026-03-17 during FIX-029 review. Fix: update line 99 of test_ins015_macos_build_jobs.py to assert len(arm_steps) == 6.

Title: _scan_python_inline_code allows bare and aliased requests imports ? Category C network scanner bypass
Description: _scan_python_inline_code uses re.search(r"\brequests\.", low) to detect requests network usage. This check requires a dot after "requests", so bare "import requests" returns True (safe) and aliased usage "import requests as r; r.get('http://evil.com')" also returns True. Neither triggers the network-module denial. Category C is supposed to block all requests network imports per AC-4 of US-027.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Call _scan_python_inline_code("import requests"). 2. Observe True (safe) ? expected False. 3. Call _scan_python_inline_code("import requests as r; r.get('http://evil.com/')"). 4. Observe True ? expected False.
Expected Behaviour: _scan_python_inline_code returns False for any code string importing the requests library.
Actual Behaviour: Returns True (safe) for bare import and aliased use. Aliased pattern fully bypasses the network filter allowing unrestricted HTTP access.
Comments: Discovered Tester Agent 2026-03-18 during SAF-026 review. Fix: replace re.search(r"\brequests\.", low) with re.search(r"\brequests\b", low). "requirements" does NOT contain "requests" as a substring so plain word-boundary match is safe.

Title: _scan_python_inline_code allows from-import syntax for http modules ? Category C http bypass
Description: _scan_python_inline_code checks for substrings "http.client" and "http.server". Python from-import syntax "from http import server" or "from http import client" does not produce these substrings ? the module name appears as the standalone word "http" and the attribute as a separate word "server" / "client". Both patterns return True (safe) when they should return False.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Call _scan_python_inline_code("from http import server; server.HTTPServer(('', 8080), None).serve_forever()"). 2. Observe True ? expected False. 3. Call _scan_python_inline_code("from http import client; c = client.HTTPConnection('evil.com')"). 4. Observe True ? expected False.
Expected Behaviour: _scan_python_inline_code returns False for any from-import of the http module.
Actual Behaviour: Returns True for from-import patterns. Agent could spin up HTTP servers or make HTTP connections by using from-import syntax.
Comments: Discovered Tester Agent 2026-03-18 during SAF-026 review. Fix: add "http" to the Category C net list, or add re.search(r"\bhttp\b", low) check. "http" as plain substring also catches "http://..." URL strings ? acceptable under fail-closed policy.

Title: _scan_python_inline_code allows bare codecs import ? Category B obfuscation scanner bypass
Description: _scan_python_inline_code checks for the substring "codecs.decode" in Category B (obfuscation). Bare "import codecs" does not contain "codecs.decode" and returns True (safe). An agent can import the codecs module and use codecs.encode with rot_13 or zlib_codec as an obfuscation mechanism without triggering any denial.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Call _scan_python_inline_code("import codecs"). 2. Observe True (safe) ? expected False. 3. Call _scan_python_inline_code("import codecs; codecs.encode('x', 'rot_13')"). 4. Observe True ? expected False.
Expected Behaviour: _scan_python_inline_code returns False for any code string importing the codecs module.
Actual Behaviour: Returns True for bare codecs import and codecs.encode usage. Agent can use codec transforms for obfuscation.
Comments: Discovered Tester Agent 2026-03-18 during SAF-026 review. Fix: change "codecs.decode" to "codecs" in the Category B pattern list. No false-positive risk ? "codecs" is a narrow, specific token.

Title: macOS codesign fails on non-code files in PyInstaller bundle
Description: After FIX-037 removed .dist-info directories, codesign --force --sign - on the .app bundle now fails on TS-Logo.png with "code object is not signed at all". Root cause is systemic: PyInstaller places all files (code AND data like images, .pyc, .zip) into Contents/MacOS/_internal/. macOS codesign expects Contents/MacOS/ to contain only code objects and treats non-code files as unsigned subcomponents. Fixing TS-Logo.png alone would just expose the next non-code file. The .app bundle-level signing must be removed in favor of component-level signing only.
Actual Behaviour: FIX-038
Fixed In WP: Closed by FIX-038 Tester PASS 2026-03-18. Systemic fix — bundle-level codesign removed; component-level signing sufficient for macOS Apple Silicon execution.

Title: Version label shows old version after update on Windows
Description: After a successful in-app update on Windows 11, the version label in the GUI still shows the previous version instead of the newly installed version. Root cause: the old process never terminates (see BUG-074) so the UI continues displaying the stale VERSION constant from the old binary.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Open launcher v2.0.x. 2. Update available banner shows v2.1.0. 3. Click Download & Install Update. 4. Update completes. 5. Version label still shows v2.0.x.
Expected Behaviour: Version label shows the newly installed version after update.
Actual Behaviour: Version label shows the old version because the old process never exits.
Comments: Symptom of BUG-074. Once the process properly exits and restarts, the new binary will display the correct version.

Title: Windows update does not restart the app
Description: After a successful in-app update on Windows 11 the app does not restart. The user must manually close and reopen the app. Two root causes: (1) _apply_windows() in applier.py calls sys.exit(0) from a daemon thread — sys.exit() from a non-main thread only raises SystemExit in that thread and does NOT terminate the main process (tkinter mainloop continues). (2) setup.iss [Run] section has skipifsilent flag — when the installer runs in /SILENT mode the [Run] entry that relaunches the app is skipped.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Open launcher. 2. Click Download & Install Update. 3. Installer runs silently (files updated). 4. App says it will restart. 5. App does NOT restart — old process continues running. 6. User must manually close and reopen.
Expected Behaviour: App terminates the old instance and relaunches the new version automatically after update.
Actual Behaviour: App does not exit (sys.exit from daemon thread is no-op for main thread) and installer's [Run] relaunch is skipped (skipifsilent).
Comments: Tested on Windows 11. Both causes must be fixed: (1) use os._exit(0) or schedule exit on main thread, (2) remove skipifsilent from setup.iss [Run].

Title: codesign on launcher inside .app triggers bundle validation — fails on TS-Logo.png
Description: codesign --force --sign - on Contents/MacOS/launcher triggers bundle-level validation because launcher is the CFBundleExecutable declared in Info.plist. codesign recursively validates all subcomponents in Contents/MacOS/ and fails on TS-Logo.png with "code object is not signed at all". FIX-038 removed .app bundle signing but kept individual launcher signing inside the .app path which has the same effect. PyInstaller already ad-hoc signs the launcher at build time (log: Re-signing the EXE). The fix is to skip re-signing and verification of the launcher at its .app bundle path and instead verify the pre-bundle binary at dist/launcher/launcher.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Push v2.1.0 tag. 2. CI runs build_dmg.sh. 3. Step 3.5 signs .dylib .so Python.framework successfully. 4. codesign --force --sign - dist/AgentEnvironmentLauncher.app/Contents/MacOS/launcher fails. 5. Error: code object is not signed at all In subcomponent: TS-Logo.png
Expected Behaviour: CI build completes without errors. All code objects are signed. Launcher retains its PyInstaller ad-hoc signature.
Actual Behaviour: codesign fails with exit code 1 on launcher inside .app because it validated TS-Logo.png as an unsigned subcomponent.
Comments: Continuation of BUG-071. FIX-038 removed .app bundle signing but signing the CFBundleExecutable inside Contents/MacOS/ has the same effect — codesign treats it as a bundle operation.

Title: .dist-info directory causes macOS codesign failure in CI
Description: agent_environment_launcher-2.1.0.dist-info directory inside the .app bundle's _internal/ folder causes codesign to fail with "bundle format unrecognized, invalid, or unsuitable". PyInstaller bundles .dist-info directories from pip install -e . which are not needed at runtime and not valid bundle components for macOS code signing.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Run pip install -e . in CI. 2. Run pyinstaller launcher.spec. 3. Run build_dmg.sh. 4. Observe codesign error: dist/AgentEnvironmentLauncher.app/Contents/MacOS/launcher: bundle format unrecognized, invalid, or unsuitable In subcomponent: .../dist/AgentEnvironmentLauncher.app/Contents/MacOS/_internal/agent_environment_launcher-2.1.0.dist-info
Expected Behaviour: macOS CI build completes without codesign errors. No .dist-info directories remain in the .app bundle.
Actual Behaviour: codesign fails with "bundle format unrecognized, invalid, or unsuitable" due to .dist-info directory in _internal/ folder. CI build cannot produce a signed artifact.
Comments: Reported by CI Pipeline 2026-03-18. .dist-info directories are Python package metadata installed by pip install -e . and included in the PyInstaller bundle. They are not needed at runtime and cannot be processed by macOS codesign as bundle subcomponents.

Title: Version label shows stale .dist-info version after overlay install
Description: get_display_version() calls importlib.metadata.version() which reads from .dist-info directories bundled by PyInstaller. When the Inno Setup installer overlays a new version over an old one both .dist-info directories coexist in _internal/. importlib.metadata finds the old one first and returns the stale version. Meanwhile check_for_update() uses VERSION constant directly (correct). The version label therefore shows the old version even though the new binary is running.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Install v2.1.1. 2. Update to v2.1.2 via in-app update. 3. App restarts (FIX-040 working). 4. Version label shows v2.1.1. 5. Check for Updates says You are up to date.
Expected Behaviour: Version label shows v2.1.2 after updating to v2.1.2.
Actual Behaviour: Version label shows v2.1.1 because importlib.metadata reads stale .dist-info from the old installation.
Comments: Root cause: importlib.metadata is unreliable in PyInstaller overlay installs. Fix: skip importlib.metadata in bundled builds and always use VERSION constant.

Title: NoAgentZone hidden from VS Code file explorer
Description: SAF-022 added NoAgentZone to files.exclude which hides it from the VS Code file explorer. Human users cannot see or access the folder through the IDE. NoAgentZone should be visible to humans but remain excluded from search and blocked by the security gate.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Open a project workspace in VS Code. 2. Look at file explorer. 3. NoAgentZone folder is not visible.
Expected Behaviour: NoAgentZone is visible in the file explorer but still excluded from search and blocked by the security gate.
Actual Behaviour: NoAgentZone is hidden from the file explorer due to files.exclude setting.
Comments: SAF-022 was overly aggressive ? files.exclude hides from human users too. search.exclude is sufficient for agent search protection.

Title: ts-python shim timed out after 5 seconds on all platforms
Description: verify_ts_python() in shim_config.py invokes .cmd files via subprocess.run without cmd.exe /c causing hangs on Windows ARM. The 5-second timeout is too short for x64 Python emulation on ARM. The CI step to download Python embeddable was commented out meaning installers shipped without bundled Python.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Install v3.0.0 on Windows 11 ARM. 2. Open launcher. 3. Click Create Project. 4. See error: bundled Python runtime is not accessible. Details: ts-python shim timed out after 5 seconds.
Expected Behaviour: Project creation succeeds without timeout error.
Actual Behaviour: Timeout error shown; launcher unusable on Windows ARM and potentially other slow platforms.
Comments: Reported post v3.0.0 release. Three root causes: (1) no cmd.exe /c for .cmd invocation (2) 5-second timeout too short for ARM (3) python-embed CI step was commented out.

Title: FIX-048 broke 6 SAF-034 tests that checked old shim_config behavior
Description: FIX-048 changed verify_ts_python() behavior: added cmd.exe /c wrapper for .cmd shims on Windows and changed timeout from 5 to 30 seconds. However the developer did NOT update the SAF-034 regression tests that validate those specific behaviors. Six tests in tests/SAF-034/ now fail: (1) test_verify_ts_python_windows_uses_shim_dir_when_exists asserts args_used[0]==shim_path but now it is cmd.exe (2) test_verify_ts_python_windows_fallback_to_path same issue (3) test_verify_ts_python_windows_shim_dir_takes_precedence same (4) test_verify_ts_python_timeout_message_mentions_5_seconds checks for 5 seconds but message now says 30 seconds (5) test_verify_ts_python_passes_timeout_5_to_subprocess expects timeout=5 but it is now 30 (6) test_verify_ts_python_shim_path_with_spaces checks args_used[0]==shim_path but is now cmd.exe.
Steps to Reproduce: Run: python -m pytest tests/SAF-034/ --tb=short  All 6 listed tests fail with AssertionError.
Expected Behaviour: SAF-034 tests pass reflecting the CURRENT (correct) behavior of verify_ts_python().
Actual Behaviour: SAF-034 tests fail because they encode assumptions about OLD behavior (no cmd.exe wrapper / timeout=5) that FIX-048 intentionally changed.
Comments: Introduced by FIX-048 developer who changed shim_config.py but did not update pre-existing SAF-034 tests. Developer must update tests/SAF-034/test_saf034.py and tests/SAF-034/test_saf034_edge.py.

Title: FIX-048 broke 6 FIX-047 tests that check for version 3.0.0
Description: FIX-047 version bump tests check that all 5 version files contain 3.0.0. FIX-048 bumped the version from 3.0.0 to 3.0.1. The FIX-047 tests now fail because the version strings no longer match. This is the same pattern that recurs with every version-bump WP in this project.
Steps to Reproduce: Run: python -m pytest tests/FIX-047/  6 tests fail: test_config_py_version / test_pyproject_toml_version / test_setup_iss_version / test_build_dmg_sh_version / test_build_appimage_sh_version / test_version_consistency.
Expected Behaviour: FIX-047 tests should be updated to reflect current version (3.0.1) OR the project should adopt a version-agnostic test strategy.
Actual Behaviour: FIX-047 tests fail because they hardcode version 3.0.0 which was superseded by FIX-048.

Title: INS-011 _apply_windows uses os._exit(0) which kills the pytest process
Description: _apply_windows() in src/launcher/core/applier.py calls os._exit(0) instead of sys.exit(0). The INS-011 test suite patches sys.exit but NOT os._exit. When tests/INS-011/test_ins011_applier.py::TestWindowsApply runs _apply_windows(), os._exit(0) terminates the entire Python/pytest process immediately. This causes all tests after INS-011 in the full suite to never run. Exit code from os._exit(0) is 0 so the partial run appears to succeed.
Steps to Reproduce: Run: python3 -m pytest tests/ — output stops at 49% (tests/INS-011/test_ins011_applier.py::TestWindowsApply::test_windows_popen_called_with_list_args) and exit code is 0. Full suite never completes.
Expected Behaviour: Full test suite runs to completion. tests/INS-011/test_ins011_applier.py patches os._exit so that _apply_windows can be tested without killing the process.
Actual Behaviour: pytest process is killed by os._exit(0) when TestWindowsApply tests run. Tests after INS-011 in alphabetical order (SAF-*, FIX-049, etc.) are silently skipped.
Comments: Closed 2026-03-20. Fixed in FIX-053 Iteration 2: patch(launcher.core.applier.os._exit) added to all _apply_windows call sites in tests/INS-011/. 66/66 INS-011 tests pass. Full suite completes past INS-011.

Title: ts-python.cmd cmd.exe parsing error with paths containing spaces
Description: ts-python.cmd uses %PYTHON_PATH% unquoted inside if ( ) blocks. When python-path.txt contains a path with spaces (e.g. C:\Program Files\Agent Environment Launcher\python-embed\python.exe) cmd.exe expands %PYTHON_PATH% at parse-time before determining block structure. Combined with the 1>&2 redirect and echo text the parser breaks producing exit code 255 and 'Agent was unexpected at this time' error. Additionally verify_ts_python() passes print(sys.version) through cmd.exe /c which contains parentheses that can interfere with cmd.exe parsing.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Install v3.0.1 to C:\Program Files\Agent Environment Launcher\. 2. Open launcher. 3. Click Create Project. 4. Error dialog: Python Runtime Unavailable — ts-python exited with code 255: \Agent was unexpected at this time.
Expected Behaviour: Project creation proceeds normally.
Actual Behaviour: ts-python exited with code 255. Error: \Agent was unexpected at this time. Project cannot be created.
Comments: Introduced by FIX-048 (v3.0.1) which added cmd.exe /c wrapper for .cmd invocation.

Title: FIX-050 broke INS-019 test_windows_shim_python_path_is_quoted
Description: FIX-050 rewrote ts-python.cmd to use !PYTHON_PATH! (delayed expansion) throughout. tests/INS-019/test_ins019_edge_cases.py::test_windows_shim_python_path_is_quoted asserts '\"%PYTHON_PATH%\' in content — this was the correct assertion before FIX-050 but is now stale. The underlying requirement (path is double-quoted) is still met by '\"!PYTHON_PATH!\"' but the test literal needs updating."
Steps to Reproduce: Run: python -m pytest tests/INS-019/test_ins019_edge_cases.py::test_windows_shim_python_path_is_quoted. Test fails with AssertionError: Windows shim must invoke Python path with double quotes.
Expected Behaviour: Test passes after updating assertion to check for '""!PYTHON_PATH!""' instead of '""%" "PYTHON_PATH%""'.
Actual Behaviour: test fails because it hardcodes the old %PYTHON_PATH% syntax.
Comments: Discovered by Tester Agent during FIX-050 review 2026-03-19. Fix: update tests/INS-019/test_ins019_edge_cases.py line ~95 to assert '"!PYTHON_PATH!"' in content.

Title: FIX-050 introduced 13+ new SAF-034 failures via read_python_path() first step
Description: FIX-050 rewrote verify_ts_python() to call read_python_path() as its FIRST step. SAF-034 tests were written for the old shim-first interface and mock shutil.which + subprocess.run but NOT read_python_path(). With the new implementation read_python_path() returns None (no mock) so verify_ts_python() returns (False 'Python path configuration not found.') before reaching the subprocess step. SAF-034 failures increased from 6 (BUG-078 baseline from FIX-048) to 19 after FIX-050.
Steps to Reproduce: Run: python -m pytest tests/SAF-034/ --tb=short. 19 tests fail vs 6 pre-existing (BUG-078). Newly failing tests include test_verify_ts_python_success_unix / test_verify_ts_python_nonzero_exit / test_verify_ts_python_timeout / test_verify_ts_python_os_error / test_verify_ts_python_unix_uses_which and others.
Expected Behaviour: SAF-034 has at most 6 failures (BUG-078 pre-existing). All new tests mock read_python_path() and python_path.exists() correctly.
Actual Behaviour: 19 SAF-034 tests fail (13 newly introduced by FIX-050 plus 6 pre-existing from BUG-078).
Comments: Discovered by Tester Agent during FIX-050 review 2026-03-19. Fix: add patch.object(sc 'read_python_path' return_value=fake_python) to each newly-failing SAF-034 test. fake_python must be created (not just referenced) so python_path.exists() returns True.

Title: FIX-048 test suite has 12 stale tests asserting removed cmd.exe wrapper behavior
Description: FIX-050 removed the cmd.exe /c wrapper from verify_ts_python() entirely. The 12 failing tests in tests/FIX-048/test_fix048.py assert the old cmd.exe-based invocation (args[0]=="cmd.exe") and do not mock read_python_path(). With the new implementation read_python_path() returns None (no mock) so verify_ts_python() exits early before reaching subprocess. Pattern is identical to the SAF-034 regression fixed in FIX-050 Iteration 2 but the FIX-048 test suite was not updated.
Steps to Reproduce: Run: python -m pytest tests/FIX-048/ --tb=short. 12 tests fail with assert False is True or NoneType not subscriptable.
Expected Behaviour: FIX-048 tests updated to mock read_python_path() and test the new direct-Python behavior. All 21 FIX-048 tests pass.
Actual Behaviour: 12 tests fail because they encode removed cmd.exe wrapper assumptions and lack read_python_path() mock.

Fix: update tests/FIX-048/test_fix048.py — add read_python_path() mock and update subprocess arg assertions from [cmd.exe /c shim ...] to [python_path -c ...].

Title: FIX-055 re-introduces ArchitecturesAllowed into setup.iss breaking FIX-012 and INS-005 regression guards
Description: FIX-055 adds ArchitecturesInstallIn64BitMode=x64compatible and ArchitecturesAllowed=x64compatible to [Setup] in setup.iss. FIX-012 was created specifically to fix BUG-041 (CI Inno Setup compilation failure from ArchitecturesAllowed) and added regression tests asserting these directives must be absent. FIX-055 violates those guards without updating the stale tests in FIX-012 and INS-005. Additionally INS-018 and INS-021 have regression tests that assert PythonEmbedExists must exist — also violated by FIX-055 without update.
Steps to Reproduce: Run full test suite after FIX-055 is applied: python -m pytest tests/FIX-012 tests/INS-005 tests/INS-018 tests/INS-021 — 6 tests fail.
Expected Behaviour: FIX-012 and INS-005 tests updated to reflect that the new Inno Setup 6 directives (ArchitecturesInstallIn64BitMode / ArchitecturesAllowed) are now confirmed to be supported by CI. INS-018 and INS-021 tests updated to assert PythonEmbedExists is absent.
Actual Behaviour: 6 test regressions: FIX-012 x2 + INS-005 x1 + INS-018 x2 + INS-021 x1.
Comments: Discovered by Tester Agent during FIX-055 review 2026-03-19. Developer must update stale tests in FIX-012 INS-005 INS-018 and INS-021 before FIX-055 can be approved.

Title: FIX-050 version tests hardcode 3.0.2 — fails after version bump to 3.0.3
Description: tests/FIX-050/test_fix050.py contains 5 tests that assert the hardcoded version string 3.0.2 against all canonical version files (config.py, pyproject.toml, setup.iss, build_dmg.sh, build_appimage.sh). After the version was bumped to 3.0.3 by a subsequent WP, these 5 tests now fail every run. This is the same recurrent hardcoded-version pattern documented in BUG-071, BUG-079. test_config_py_version asserts CURRENT_VERSION == '3.0.2', test_pyproject_toml_version asserts version = '3.0.2', etc.
Steps to Reproduce: Run: python -m pytest tests/FIX-050/ --tb=short. Observe 5 failures: test_config_py_version, test_pyproject_toml_version, test_setup_iss_version, test_build_dmg_sh_version, test_build_appimage_sh_version.
Expected Behaviour: FIX-050 version tests pass after any version bump by using CURRENT_VERSION from version_utils dynamically.
Actual Behaviour: 5 tests fail: expected '3.0.2' but current version is '3.0.3'.

Title: FIX-053 incomplete: two tests in test_ins011_tester_edge_cases.py missing os._exit mock
Description: FIX-053 patched os._exit in test_ins011_applier.py::TestNoShellTrue.test_windows_subprocess_no_shell_true but missed two identical defects in test_ins011_tester_edge_cases.py::TestPathEdgeCases. Both test_windows_path_with_spaces (line 109) and test_windows_path_with_unicode (line 131) call _apply_windows() with only patch('launcher.core.applier.sys.exit') active. Since _apply_windows() calls os._exit(0) (not sys.exit), the real os._exit fires and kills the pytest process at test 47/66.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Run: .venv/Scripts/python -m pytest tests/INS-011/ -v. 2. Observe pytest is killed at TestPathEdgeCases.test_windows_path_with_spaces (test 47/66). 3. Tests 47-66 never run.
Expected Behaviour: All 66 tests in tests/INS-011/ complete. pytest survives to the end of the suite.
Actual Behaviour: pytest process is killed by os._exit(0) at test 47/66. test_windows_path_with_spaces and test_windows_path_with_unicode in test_ins011_tester_edge_cases.py both lack patch('launcher.core.applier.os._exit').
Comments: Closed 2026-03-20. Fixed in FIX-053 Iteration 2: patch(launcher.core.applier.os._exit) added to both test_windows_path_with_spaces and test_windows_path_with_unicode. All 66 INS-011 tests pass.

Title: macOS codesign fails on TS-Logo.png non-code subcomponent in Contents/MacOS/_internal/
Description: macOS CI build fails during ad-hoc code signing. codesign --options runtime encounters TS-Logo.png (a non-code PNG file) inside Contents/MacOS/_internal/ and rejects it as an unsigned subcomponent. Error: 'code object is not signed at all / In subcomponent: .../TS-Logo.png'. Root cause: build_dmg.sh copies all PyInstaller onedir output (including data files like TS-Logo.png and TS-Logo.ico) into Contents/MacOS/. macOS code signing expects only executable code in Contents/MacOS/; non-code resources belong in Contents/Resources/.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Push a version tag to trigger CI. 2. Observe macos-arm-build job fails at code signing step. 3. Error message: 'code object is not signed at all / In subcomponent: TS-Logo.png'.
Expected Behaviour: Code signing succeeds; DMG is produced.
Actual Behaviour: codesign fails with exit code 1 due to TS-Logo.png being treated as an unsigned code subcomponent.

Title: macOS codesign fails on any non-code file in Contents/MacOS/_internal/ (customtkinter .ico, etc.)
Description: FIX-062 relocated TS-Logo.png/ico but the same codesign failure recurs for other non-code files deeper in _internal/, e.g. customtkinter/assets/icons/CustomTkinter_icon_Windows.ico. The root cause is structural: ALL of _internal/ lives inside Contents/MacOS/, so codesign treats every file as a code subcomponent. Targeting individual files is whack-a-mole. The correct fix is to move the entire _internal/ directory to Contents/Resources/_internal/ and replace Contents/MacOS/_internal with a symlink. macOS codesign does NOT traverse symlinks—it records them as symlinks in CodeResources. This means Contents/MacOS/ only contains the launcher executable, and no non-code file can trigger the error.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Push v3.1.0 tag. 2. macos-arm-build job fails at code signing step. 3. Error: code object is not signed at all / In subcomponent: customtkinter/assets/icons/CustomTkinter_icon_Windows.ico
Expected Behaviour: Code signing succeeds; DMG is produced.
Actual Behaviour: codesign fails with exit code 1 due to non-code .ico file in _internal/ treated as unsigned subcomponent.

Title: FIX-063 removes Step 3.2 but does not update tests/FIX-062 test suite
Description: FIX-063 intentionally removes Step 3.2 (the per-file TS-Logo.png/ico relocation loop introduced by FIX-062) because the entire _internal/ directory is now moved to Contents/Resources/. However, the developer did not update tests/FIX-062/test_fix062_resource_relocation.py. 16 tests in that file now FAIL because they assert Step 3.2 is present (checking for the header comment, the for-loop, mv/ln-s patterns, step ordering, synlink depth, etc.). The FIX-063 implementation is architecturally correct, but the test suite is not kept in sync.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Check out FIX-063/relocate-internal-to-resources branch. 2. Run: .venv/Scripts/python -m pytest tests/FIX-062/ -v. 3. Observe 16 failures in test_fix062_resource_relocation.py.
Expected Behaviour: tests/FIX-062/test_fix062_resource_relocation.py updated to reflect that Step 3.2 is intentionally absent: the 16 Step-3.2-presence tests replaced with Step-3.2-ABSENCE assertions, symlink depth updated from 2 to 1, TS-Logo assertions removed.
Actual Behaviour: 16 tests fail asserting Step 3.2 presence. Suite result: 6 passed / 16 failed.
Comments: Discovered during FIX-063 Tester review 2026-03-20 (Iteration 1). FIX-028 and FIX-031 failures also apparent but confirmed pre-existing (same multi-line command structure failed on HEAD~1).

Title: action-tracker.json: Open actions have non-empty resolved_by (schema contract violation)
Description: The _schema key in action-tracker.json says resolved_by should be 'empty if Open'. However, 5 actions (ACT-006, ACT-007, ACT-009, ACT-010, ACT-011) have status=Open but non-empty resolved_by values pointing to planned WP-IDs.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Read docs/maintenance/action-tracker.json. 2. Look at actions with status=Open. 3. Check resolved_by field values.
Expected Behaviour: Open actions have empty string for resolved_by per schema documentation.
Actual Behaviour: ACT-006/007/009/010/011 are Open but have resolved_by='FIX-067', 'FIX-065', 'FIX-066', 'FIX-068' respectively.
Comments: Fix: Either (a) add a 'planned_wp' field to the schema for tracking planned resolution, and clear resolved_by for Open items; OR (b) update the _schema description to 'empty if unassigned'.

Title: security_gate.py fails to import zone_classifier with embedded Python
Description: When ts-python (embedded Python distribution) invokes security_gate.py, the import zone_classifier on line 18 fails with ModuleNotFoundError. The Python embeddable distribution ships with a ._pth file that restricts sys.path and does NOT automatically add the script directory. This means the security gate is completely non-functional in v3.1.1 — no script execution at all.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Install v3.1.1 launcher  2. Create a workspace  3. Open workspace in VS Code  4. Trigger any agent tool use  5. Observe security_gate.py crashes with ModuleNotFoundError
Expected Behaviour: security_gate.py imports zone_classifier and processes the tool request
Actual Behaviour: ModuleNotFoundError: No module named zone_classifier — security gate crashes, no protection

Title: DOC-008 breaks DOC-003 placeholder count tests (regression)
Description: DOC-008 adds a second {{PROJECT_NAME}} in the directive line, violating DOC-003 exactly-one assertion
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Checkout DOC-008 branch 2. Run tests/DOC-003/test_doc003_edge_cases.py
Expected Behaviour: {{PROJECT_NAME}} appears exactly once; test passes
Actual Behaviour: {{PROJECT_NAME}} appears twice (directive + workspace rules); 3 DOC-003 tests fail

Title: SAF-035 counter causes SAF-024 test regressions
Description: SAF-035 denial counter modifies deny message format in main(). Tests calling main() without mocking counter get Block N/M prefix or Session locked message instead of exact generic deny message.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Run pytest tests/SAF-024/ 2. tests that assert reason == _GENERIC_MESSAGE fail
Expected Behaviour: Deny message equals _GENERIC_MESSAGE exactly
Actual Behaviour: Deny message is 'Block N of 20. Access denied...' or 'Session locked — 20 denied actions reached.'
Comments: Developer must update SAF-024 tests to mock counter or check _GENERIC_MESSAGE is a substring

Title: Counter writes .hook_state.json to template directory during tests
Description: When tests import security_gate from templates/coding/ and call main(), the counter writes .hook_state.json to the template scripts directory. This pollutes the template, accumulates state across test runs, and makes SAF-024 tests non-deterministic (first 20 get Block N/M, rest get Session locked).
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Delete templates/coding/.github/hooks/scripts/.hook_state.json, 2. Run pytest tests/SAF-024/, 3. Observe .hook_state.json created with deny_count accumulating
Expected Behaviour: No files written to templates/ during testing
Actual Behaviour: .hook_state.json with accumulated deny_count written to template directory
Comments: SAF-024 and other tests that call main() need to mock _load_state, _save_state, _get_session_id. Consider conftest fixture.

Title: reset_hook_counter: os.replace() PermissionError on Windows when concurrent threads write same state file
Description: On Windows, when multiple threads call reset_counters() concurrently and race to replace the same .hook_state.json file, os.replace() raises PermissionError(13) Access is denied. The script has no file-level mutex. This is documented in the dev-log Known Limitations. Normal single-threaded usage is unaffected.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Create a .hook_state.json with multiple sessions. 2. Call reset_counters() from 5+ concurrent threads simultaneously with the same state_path. 3. On Windows, observe PermissionError: [WinError 5] Access is denied.
Expected Behaviour: All threads complete without exception, final state file is valid JSON.
Actual Behaviour: On Windows, some threads raise PermissionError(13) when os.replace() collides with another thread writing to the same destination file.
Comments: xfail test added in tests/SAF-037/test_saf037_edge_cases.py::TestConcurrentResetAttempts::test_concurrent_resets_no_exception. No file-level lock is in scope for SAF-037; a future WP can add Windows-compatible locking (e.g. msvcrt.locking or atomicfilewrite).

Title: SAF-039: percent-encoded path traversal bypasses zone check in LSP tool URIs
Description: In _extract_lsp_file_path(), when the tool payload contains a file:// URI with percent-encoded path traversal sequences (e.g. %2E%2E for ..), the path is returned without decoding. zone_classifier.classify() receives the raw percent-encoded string (e.g. /workspace/project/%2E%2E/.github/config). posixpath.normpath does not resolve %2E%2E (only resolves literal ..) so the path appears to start with /workspace/project/ and is classified as 'allow'. VS Code resolves percent-encoding before executing the tool, so the actual file operation targets .github/config, bypassing the zone restriction. Affects both vscode_listCodeUsages and vscode_renameSymbol. vscode_renameSymbol is particularly critical as it is write-like.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Construct a vscode_listCodeUsages payload with uri='file:///workspace/project/%2E%2E/.github/hooks/scripts/security_gate.py'. 2. Call sg.validate_vscode_list_code_usages(data, '/workspace'). 3. Observe 'allow' is returned. 4. VS Code would decode %2E%2E to .. and resolve the path to /workspace/.github/hooks/scripts/security_gate.py.
Expected Behaviour: validate_vscode_list_code_usages and validate_vscode_rename_symbol return 'deny' for any URI containing percent-encoded path traversal sequences that would resolve to a denied zone.
Actual Behaviour: _extract_lsp_file_path returns the raw URI path without decoding percent-encoding. posixpath.normpath leaves %2E%2E unresolved. Zone classifier sees /workspace/project/%2e%2e/.github/config, matches project/ prefix, and returns 'allow'. Bypass confirmed for .github/, .vscode/, and NoAgentZone/ targets.
Comments: Fix: call urllib.parse.unquote(path) on the extracted URI path before returning from _extract_lsp_file_path. This decodes %2E to . and %2F to / so that posixpath.normpath can then resolve the traversal and zone_classifier correctly denies it. Tester tests in tests/SAF-039/test_saf039_tester_edge_cases.py::TestPercentEncodedTraversalBypass (7 tests) all fail with current implementation.

Title: fc /flag Windows-style slash flags misidentified as paths
Description: Windows fc command uses slash-prefixed flags (/L, /B, /N, /C etc.). The _is_path_like() function in security_gate.py matches any token containing '/' via _PATH_LIKE_RE. Tokens like /L and /B trigger the path-like detection and are zone-checked as absolute paths, resolving outside the project folder and causing deny.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Run sanitize_terminal_command('fc /L project/a.txt project/b.txt', ws_root). 2. Observe deny result. 3. /L is treated as absolute path /L, not as a Windows flag.
Expected Behaviour: fc /L project/a.txt project/b.txt is allowed when both file arguments are inside the project folder.
Actual Behaviour: fc /L project/a.txt project/b.txt is denied because /L is misidentified as an absolute path outside the project folder by _is_path_like().
Comments: Discovered during SAF-040 Tester review. Same issue would affect any Windows-style /flag argument for other commands. Workaround: use fc without /L or /B flags. Marked as xfail in tests/SAF-040/test_saf040_tester_edge_cases.py.

Title: user_settings.save_settings: os.replace() PermissionError on Windows under concurrent writes
Description: On Windows, when multiple threads call save_settings() (or set_setting()) concurrently targeting the same settings file, os.replace() raises PermissionError(13) Access is denied when two threads race to rename their temp files to the same destination. The module has no file-level mutex. Normal single-threaded usage is unaffected; the file is never left in a corrupt state (atomic write guarantees this).
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Patch _settings_path() to a temp dir. 2. Launch 5 threads each calling set_setting() 20 times concurrently. 3. On Windows observe PermissionError(13) from os.replace().
Expected Behaviour: All threads complete without exception; final settings file is valid JSON.
Actual Behaviour: On Windows, some threads raise PermissionError(13) when os.replace() collides with another thread renaming to the same target path. File on disk is never corrupted (atomic write ensures this).
Comments: Known limitation documented in tester edge-case test test_concurrent_saves_file_stays_valid_json. Same root cause as BUG-096 (SAF-037). Future WP could add a threading.Lock or use msvcrt.locking for Windows-safe concurrent access.

Title: DOC-010 test uses sliding git range HEAD~2..HEAD causing false failure after any src/ commit
Description: tests/DOC-010/test_doc010_tester_edge_cases.py::TestSourceCodeUnmodified::test_src_directory_not_modified_by_wp uses 'git diff --name-only HEAD~2 HEAD -- src/' to check if DOC-010 (research WP) modified src/. The relative HEAD~2..HEAD window means the test passes only when the 2 most-recent commits are unrelated to src/, then fails whenever any future WP commits src/ changes. It is not testing DOC-010 commits; it is testing arbitrary recent history.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Commit any legitimate feature WP that modifies src/. 2. Run tests/DOC-010/test_doc010_tester_edge_cases.py. 3. Test fails asserting DOC-010 modified src/, even though DOC-010 did not.
Expected Behaviour: Test only checks commits from the DOC-010 branch, not recent history relative to current HEAD.
Actual Behaviour: Test fails after GUI-019 commits src/launcher/gui/app.py changes even though DOC-010 never touched src/.
Comments: Surfaced during GUI-019 tester review. Fix: pin the git range to DOC-010's actual commit SHA or the merge-base of the DOC-010 branch.

Title: file_search allows relative .git/ queries not in search.exclude
Description: validate_file_search only name-checks .github, .vscode, noagentzone. Relative queries like **/.git/** or .git/config are allowed. VS Code search.exclude does not exclude .git/.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Invoke file_search with query '**/.git/**'. 2. Observe gate returns allow.
Expected Behaviour: Gate denies .git/ queries; search.exclude prevents .git/ from appearing in results.
Actual Behaviour: Gate allows relative .git/ queries. search.exclude has no .git/ entry.
Comments: Absolute .git/ paths are correctly denied by zone_classifier default-deny. Low risk. SAF-032 blocks direct read_file access to .git/. Follow-on WP recommended. Deferred — Low severity. .git/ is already excluded by VS Code default settings. Will address if elevated.

Title: validate_search_subagent allows percent-encoded zone names (%2egithub)
Description: validate_search_subagent uses raw literal string matching for deny-zone names. Percent-encoded variants such as %2egithub (for .github) or %2evscode (for .vscode) bypass the substring check because the literal string .github is absent from the encoded form. The validator returns allow for these queries. The underlying VS Code search.exclude configuration provides the real protection — the semantic index and search_subagent infrastructure do not surface .github or .vscode content regardless — but the gate-level check is incomplete.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Construct a search_subagent query where the deny zone name is percent-encoded: %2egithub/hooks.py. 2. Call sg.validate_search_subagent({'query': '%2egithub/hooks.py'}, '/workspace'). 3. Observe return value is 'allow' instead of 'deny'.
Expected Behaviour: validate_search_subagent returns deny for queries containing percent-encoded deny-zone names such as %2egithub, %2evscode, or noagentzone
Actual Behaviour: validate_search_subagent returns allow because the literal string .github is not present in the raw percent-encoded query string
Comments: Discovered during SAF-044 Tester review. Low severity because VS Code search.exclude is the primary protection layer. Remediation could add URL-decode step before the deny-zone name check in a follow-up WP.
